```{r downloads, include=FALSE}

install.packages("data.table", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("dplyr", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("ggplot2", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("ggExtra", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("ggpubr", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("tidyr", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("reshape2", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("MASS", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("ggimage", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("ggsignif", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("plotly", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("vegan", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("lme4", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("lmerTest", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("stringr", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("tibble", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("readr", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("ggdendro", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("dendextend", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("ggrepel", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("RColorBrewer", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("tidyverse", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("patchwork", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("ggtext", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("apaTables", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("webshot", dependencies = TRUE)
install.packages("broom.mixed")
install.packages("performance")
install.packages("parameters") 
install.packages("smacof", dependencies = TRUE) 
```

## ______________Do these 7 steps first_____________________
## ____________Ensure you have 'outline' on_________________


## 1. Packages <- Always load at session start
```{r, library}
# This loads initial packages that are used throughout 
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggExtra)
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyr)
library(reshape2)
library(MASS)
library(ggimage)
library(ggsignif)
library(plotly)
library(vegan)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(stringr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(ggdendro)
library(dendextend)
library(ggrepel)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
```

## 2. Load Data 1
```{r pre load data}

### This is the initial loading step, it is followed by a second one, ensure that you see your .csv files here ###

## Note: Put data files you want to load in the data_for_analysis folder 
  ## All other folders are just for sorting and storage, this folder is the 'active' one. 

all_files <- list.files(path = '/Users/gleim/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/University/2023:24 Honours/Project Stats and R/Motion_analysis_KR/data/data_for_analysis', pattern = ".csv$")

# Folder Path
folder_path <- '/Users/gleim/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/University/2023:24 Honours/Project Stats and R/Motion_analysis_KR/data/data_for_analysis'

print(all_files)

```
## 3. Load Data 2

```{r load data}
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)

###   Cleans up the data by selecting only the stimulus parameters I care about
###   Ensure that you change these so that it finds your variables. 

# List all CSV files in the folder
file_names <- list.files(path = folder_path, pattern = "*.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Specify the columns to keep
columns_to_keep <- c("participant", "circle1_ori", "circle2_ori", "circle1_SF", "circle2_SF", "similarity", "date", "response_time")

# Initialize an empty list to store data tables
list_of_data_tables <- list()

# Iterate over each file, read the specific columns, and add it to the list
for (file in file_names) {
  # Use fread to read the file, selecting only the specified columns
  current_data <- fread(file, select = columns_to_keep)
  
  # Add the current data table to the list
  list_of_data_tables[[length(list_of_data_tables) + 1]] <- current_data
}

# Combine all data tables in the list into a single data table, removing NA
  # this is messy (sorry)
  # needed to make the 'pretimedata' frame for use in checking timestamps but
  # had to messily remove non-used columns 

data <- rbindlist(list_of_data_tables, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
pretimedata <- rbindlist(list_of_data_tables, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
data <- na.omit(data, cols = c("participant", "circle1_ori", "circle2_ori", "circle1_SF", "circle2_SF", "similarity", "response_time"))
pretimedata <- na.omit(data, cols = c("participant", "date", "circle1_ori", "circle2_ori", "circle1_SF", "circle2_SF", "similarity", "response_time"))
data <- dplyr::select(data, -date)
pretimedata <- dplyr::select(pretimedata, -similarity)

# Print the combined data table to check
print(data)

# Create 'data_for_count' by taking the first 150 rows for each participant and excluding the 'similarity' column
  # This is because the experiment has 150 trials x 2 (for Double Pass), change if your exp. is different.
data_for_count <- data[, .SD[1:150], by = participant][, !"similarity", with = FALSE]

# Print the new data table to check
print(data_for_count)

# Optional: Check the structure and summary of 'data_for_count'
str(data_for_count)
summary(data_for_count)

# Check the structure and summary of the combined data
str(data)
summary(data)

```
## 3.5 Rescale to dissimilarity before correlation 
```{r, pre-rescale }
  # First rescale similarity to dissimilarity 
  str(data)
  table(data$similarity)
  
  rescale <- function(data) {
  # Ensure that similarity is numeric
  if (!is.numeric(data$similarity)) {
    data$similarity <- as.numeric(as.character(data$similarity))
  }
  
  # Multiply by -1
  data$similarity <- data$similarity * -1
  
  # Apply conditional adjustments
  data$dissimilarity <- ifelse(data$similarity >= 1, data$similarity + 3, data$similarity + 4)
  
  return(data)
}

# Apply the function to  data
data <- rescale(data)
  
```


##4. Double Pass Computation and Plot

```{r, DP Correlation and plot}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

  ### This computes the double pass correlation and plots it
  ### First it selects per participant, then splits their data into two blocks 
  ### Then computes the correlation and bifurcates the data into separate data frames 
  ### Also checks the set double pass cutoff against the critical R value  

## Select the double pass correlation cutoff
dp_cutoff = 0.1603

# No. of pairwise trials before duplication for double pass 
n <- 150

# Function to calculate number of unique participants
n.participants <- function(df) {
  length(unique(df$participant))
}

# Function to calculate double-pass correlation
double.cor <- function(dissimilarity) {
  if(length(dissimilarity) != 300) {
    cat("Warning: dissimilarity vector does not have 300 elements\n")
  }
  
  first_pass <- dissimilarity[1:150]
  second_pass <- dissimilarity[151:300]
  
  # Check for constant data
  if(sd(first_pass) == 0 | sd(second_pass) == 0) {
    cat("Warning: one of the vectors is constant\n")
    return(NA)
  }
  
  ### CHECK CORR. METHOD 
  result <- cor(first_pass, second_pass, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
  
  if(is.na(result)) {
    cat("NA correlation detected\n")
  }
  
  return(result)
}

# Calculate double-pass correlations for each participant
double.cor.summary <- data %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  summarise(correlation = double.cor(dissimilarity))


# Subset based on correlation threshold
double.cor.acceptable <- filter(double.cor.summary, correlation >= dp_cutoff)
double.cor.unacceptable <- filter(double.cor.summary, correlation < dp_cutoff)

# Extract trial data for acceptable and unacceptable correlations
trialdata <- filter(data, participant %in% double.cor.acceptable$participant) 
lowdpdata <- filter(data, participant %in% double.cor.unacceptable$participant)

# Plotting function for double-pass correlations
plot_double_pass_correlation <- function(data, title, cutoff = NULL) {
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(y = correlation, x = factor(1))) +
    geom_violin(trim = FALSE, fill = "skyblue", color = "blue") +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 2.5) +
    xlab(NULL) +  ylab("Spearman's Correlation") +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)) +
    theme_pubr()+
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "plain", size = 20),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain", margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0), hjust = 0.65),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain", colour = "black"),
      axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 1.5),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "white"),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#d6d6d6"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank() 
    )

  if (!is.null(dp_cutoff)) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = dp_cutoff, linewidth = 1.5, colour = 'red', linetype = 'dashed') +
      annotate("text", x = 1.4, y = dp_cutoff - 0.07, size = 10, label = paste("Cut-off:", dp_cutoff), vjust = 0, colour = 'red')
  }
  
  print(p)
}

# Plot correlations for all data, acceptable, and unacceptable
plot_double_pass_correlation(double.cor.acceptable, "DP Correlation - Acceptable", dp_cutoff)

# Saving the plot (Example for one; repeat as necessary)
ggsave("DP_Correlation_Acceptable_Data.png", plot = last_plot(), width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

plot_double_pass_correlation(double.cor.summary, "DP Correlation - All Data", dp_cutoff)

# Saving the plot (Example for one; repeat as necessary)
ggsave("DP_Correlation_All_Data.png", plot = last_plot(), width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

# Save trial data for acceptable correlations to CSV
write.csv(trialdata, "trialdata.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(data, "data.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Print number of participants in various datasets
cat("Participants - All Data:", n.participants(data), "\n")
cat("Participants - Acceptable:", n.participants(double.cor.acceptable), "\n")
cat("Participants - Unacceptable:", n.participants(double.cor.unacceptable), "\n")

basic_stats <- double.cor.acceptable %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(correlation, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(correlation, na.rm = TRUE),
    variance = var(correlation, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(correlation, na.rm = TRUE),
    mad = mad(correlation, constant = 1, na.rm = TRUE) # Median Absolute Deviation
  )

# Display the basic statistics
print(basic_stats)



## Check cutoff value 
# Function to calculate critical r-value given DOF and alpha
critical_r_value <- function(n, alpha = 0.05) {
  dof <- n - 2
  t_critical <- qt(1 - alpha / 2, df = dof)  # Two-tailed test
  r_critical <- t_critical / sqrt(t_critical^2 + dof)
  return(r_critical)
}

# Calculate the critical r-value for this experiment
r_critical <- critical_r_value(n)

print(paste("Critical r-value for n =", n, "is:", round(r_critical, 4)))



# Calculate the critical r-value for this experiment
r_critical <- critical_r_value(n)


# Interpretation:
# Compare your cutoff to the critical r-value
if (dp_cutoff > r_critical) {
  print(paste("The chosen cutoff of", dp_cutoff,"is more stringent than the critical value."))
} else if (round(dp_cutoff, 4) == round(r_critical, 4)) { 
  print(paste("The chosen cutoff of", dp_cutoff, "is equal to the critical value."))
} else {
  print(paste("The chosen cutoff of", dp_cutoff,"is not stringent compared to the critical value."))
}
  

```

## 5. Check DP Spearman vs Pearson 
```{r}
# This is to check how participant DP correlation changes based on pearson or spearman 

# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer) # For rainbow colors
library(tidyr)
library(ggrepel) # For repelling labels with arrows
library(ggpubr)  # For theme_pubr()

# Function to calculate double-pass correlation with the specified method
double.cor <- function(dissimilarity, method = "pearson") {
  if(length(dissimilarity) != 300) {
    cat("Warning: dissimilarity vector does not have 300 elements\n")
  }
  
  first_pass <- dissimilarity[1:150]
  second_pass <- dissimilarity[151:300]
  
  # Check for constant data
  if(sd(first_pass) == 0 | sd(second_pass) == 0) {
    cat("Warning: one of the vectors is constant\n")
    return(NA)
  }
  
  result <- cor(first_pass, second_pass, method = method, use = "complete.obs")
  
  if(is.na(result)) {
    cat("NA correlation detected\n")
  }
  
  return(result)
}

# Calculate double-pass correlations for each participant using both Pearson and Spearman
double.cor.summary <- data %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  summarise(
    pearson_correlation = double.cor(dissimilarity, method = "pearson"),
    spearman_correlation = double.cor(dissimilarity, method = "spearman")
  )

# Function to calculate critical r-value given DOF and alpha
critical_r_value <- function(n, alpha = 0.05) {
  dof <- n - 2
  t_critical <- qt(1 - alpha / 2, df = dof)  # Two-tailed test
  r_critical <- t_critical / sqrt(t_critical^2 + dof)
  return(r_critical)
}

# Assuming sample size of 150 for each correlation
n <- 150
r_critical <- critical_r_value(n)

# Identify participants based on specified criteria:
# 1. Absolute change between Pearson and Spearman correlations is >= 0.1.
# 2. Significant relative change, e.g., one above and one below critical R value.
highlighted <- double.cor.summary %>%
  filter(
    abs(pearson_correlation - spearman_correlation) >= 0.1 | # Absolute change of 0.1 or more
    (pearson_correlation > r_critical & spearman_correlation < r_critical) | # Crosses critical R value: Pearson > Spearman
    (pearson_correlation < r_critical & spearman_correlation > r_critical)   # Crosses critical R value: Spearman > Pearson
  )

# Assign colors: special participants get unique colors, others get black
double.cor.summary <- double.cor.summary %>%
  mutate(
    group = ifelse(participant %in% highlighted$participant, "highlighted", "normal"),
    color = ifelse(group == "highlighted", participant, "black") # Use participant IDs for unique colors
  )

# Create a rainbow color palette for highlighted participants
highlighted_colors <- setNames(rainbow(nrow(highlighted)), highlighted$participant)

# Merge color assignments
double.cor.summary$color <- ifelse(double.cor.summary$group == "highlighted", 
                                   highlighted_colors[as.character(double.cor.summary$participant)], 
                                   "black")

# Melt data for ggplot
plot_data <- double.cor.summary %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("pearson_correlation", "spearman_correlation"), 
               names_to = "method", values_to = "correlation") %>%
  mutate(method = factor(method, levels = c("pearson_correlation", "spearman_correlation")))

# Filter data for labeling only highlighted participants on the Spearman side
highlighted_plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  filter(method == "spearman_correlation" & participant %in% highlighted$participant)

# Plot 1: Includes both black and colored participants with jittered lines and points
plot_full <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = method, y = correlation, group = participant, color = color)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = participant), alpha = 0.7, position = position_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0)) + # Jitter lines
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0)) + # Jitter points
  scale_color_identity() + # Use colors directly from the data
  geom_hline(yintercept = r_critical, size = 1.5, colour = 'red', linetype = 'dashed') +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)) + # Set y-axis ticks every 0.1
  labs(title = "Double-Pass Correlation (Pearson vs. Spearman) - All Participants", 
       x = NULL, 
       y = "Correlation Value") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12)
  ) +
  # Adding labels with arrows on the Spearman side for highlighted participants only
  geom_text_repel(
    data = highlighted_plot_data,
    aes(label = participant),
    nudge_x = 0.9, # Nudge labels further to the right
    linewidth = 3,
    direction = "y",
    segment.color = "black",
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.02, "npc"), type = "closed", angle = 15), # Black arrows
    box.padding = 0.4,
    segment.size = 0.5,
    max.overlaps = Inf
  )

# Save the full plot
ggsave("Double_Pass_Correlation_Full.png", plot = plot_full, width = 16, height = 20, dpi = 300)

# Plot 2: Includes only colored participants (highlighted ones) with jittered lines and points
plot_highlighted <- ggplot(filter(plot_data, participant %in% highlighted$participant), 
                           aes(x = method, y = correlation, group = participant, color = color)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = participant), alpha = 0.7, position = position_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0)) + # Jitter lines
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0)) + # Jitter points
  scale_color_identity() + # Use colors directly from the data
  geom_hline(yintercept = r_critical, linewidth = 1.5, colour = 'red', linetype = 'dashed') +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)) + # Set y-axis ticks every 0.1
  labs(title = "Double-Pass Correlation (Pearson vs. Spearman) - Highlighted Participants Only", 
       x = NULL, 
       y = "Correlation Value") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12)
  ) +
  # Adding labels with arrows on the Spearman side for highlighted participants only
  geom_text_repel(
    data = highlighted_plot_data,
    aes(label = participant),
    nudge_x = 0.9, # Nudge labels further to the right
    linewidth = 3,
    direction = "y",
    segment.color = "black",
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.02, "npc"), type = "closed", angle = 15), # Black arrows
    box.padding = 0.4,
    segment.size = 0.5,
    max.overlaps = Inf
  )

# Save the highlighted-only plot
ggsave("Double_Pass_Correlation_Highlighted.png", plot = plot_highlighted, width = 16, height = 20, dpi = 300)

```



## 6. Check participants 
```{r, check participants}
## This chunk is to check outliers in variance and mean dissimilarity 

# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggrepel)  # For adding labels without overlap
library(ggExtra)  # For adding marginal histograms
library(ggpubr)   # For pubr theme

# Function to calculate double-pass correlation with the specified method
double.cor <- function(dissimilarity, method = "pearson") {
  if(length(dissimilarity) != 300) {
    cat("Warning: dissimilarity vector does not have 300 elements\n")
  }
  
  first_pass <- dissimilarity[1:150]
  second_pass <- dissimilarity[151:300]
  
  # Check for constant data
  if(sd(first_pass) == 0 | sd(second_pass) == 0) {
    cat("Warning: one of the vectors is constant\n")
    return(NA)
  }
  
  result <- cor(first_pass, second_pass, method = method, use = "complete.obs")
  
  if(is.na(result)) {
    cat("NA correlation detected\n")
  }
  
  return(result)
}

# Calculate double-pass correlations for each participant using both Pearson and Spearman
double.cor.summary <- data %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  summarise(
    pearson_correlation = double.cor(dissimilarity, method = "pearson"),
    spearman_correlation = double.cor(dissimilarity, method = "spearman")
  )

# Function to calculate critical r-value given DOF and alpha
critical_r_value <- function(n, alpha = 0.05) {
  dof <- n - 2
  t_critical <- qt(1 - alpha / 2, df = dof)  # Two-tailed test
  r_critical <- t_critical / sqrt(t_critical^2 + dof)
  return(r_critical)
}

# Assuming sample size of 150 for each correlation
n <- 150
r_critical <- critical_r_value(n)

# Identify participants who cross the critical r-value between Pearson and Spearman
crossing_participants <- double.cor.summary %>%
  filter(
    (pearson_correlation > r_critical & spearman_correlation < r_critical) | # Crosses critical R value: Pearson > Spearman
    (pearson_correlation < r_critical & spearman_correlation > r_critical)   # Crosses critical R value: Spearman > Pearson
  )

# Get the list of participant IDs who meet the crossing criterion
crossing_ids <- crossing_participants$participant

# Function to plot scatterplots for individual dissimilarity ratings
plot_individual_scatter <- function(participant_id, outlier_type = NULL) {
  # Filter the data for the specific participant
  participant_data <- data %>% filter(participant == participant_id)
  
  # Create a data frame for plotting with first and second block ratings
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    first_block = participant_data$dissimilarity[1:150],
    second_block = participant_data$dissimilarity[151:300]
  )
  
  # Set title based on whether outlier_type is provided
  plot_title <- if (!is.null(outlier_type)) {
    paste("Dissimilarity Ratings for Outlier Participant", participant_id, "-", outlier_type)
  } else {
    paste("Dissimilarity Ratings that cross critical R for Participant", participant_id)
  }
  
  # Create the scatterplot comparing first and second block ratings with jitter
  plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = first_block, y = second_block)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, position = position_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0.1)) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 7), breaks = seq(0, 7, by = 1)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 7), breaks = seq(0, 7, by = 1)) +
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      x = "First Block Ratings",
      y = "Second Block Ratings"
    ) +
    theme_pubr() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 8),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12)
    )
  
  # Save the plot as a PNG file
  filename <- if (!is.null(outlier_type)) {
    paste0("Scatterplot_Outlier_Participant_", participant_id, "_", outlier_type, ".png")
  } else {
    paste0("Scatterplot_Participant_", participant_id, ".png")
  }
  
  ggsave(filename, plot = plot, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}

# Generate scatterplots for participants crossing the critical R value
lapply(crossing_ids, plot_individual_scatter)

# Compute mean and variance of dissimilarity ratings for each participant across all trials
dissimilarity_stats <- data %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_dissimilarity = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE),
    variance_dissimilarity = var(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_dissimilarity) & !is.na(variance_dissimilarity)) # Remove any participants with missing values

# Calculate the overall mean and standard deviation for both mean dissimilarity and variance
mean_dissimilarity_mean <- mean(dissimilarity_stats$mean_dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE)
mean_dissimilarity_sd <- sd(dissimilarity_stats$mean_dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE)

variance_dissimilarity_mean <- mean(dissimilarity_stats$variance_dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE)
variance_dissimilarity_sd <- sd(dissimilarity_stats$variance_dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE)

# Identify outliers and categorize them
dissimilarity_stats <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
  mutate(
    outlier_status = case_when(
      (mean_dissimilarity < (mean_dissimilarity_mean - 2 * mean_dissimilarity_sd) |
         mean_dissimilarity > (mean_dissimilarity_mean + 2 * mean_dissimilarity_sd)) &
      (variance_dissimilarity < (variance_dissimilarity_mean - 2 * variance_dissimilarity_sd) |
         variance_dissimilarity > (variance_dissimilarity_mean + 2 * variance_dissimilarity_sd)) ~ "Both Outliers",
      mean_dissimilarity < (mean_dissimilarity_mean - 2 * mean_dissimilarity_sd) |
        mean_dissimilarity > (mean_dissimilarity_mean + 2 * mean_dissimilarity_sd) ~ "Mean Outlier",
      variance_dissimilarity < (variance_dissimilarity_mean - 2 * variance_dissimilarity_sd) |
        variance_dissimilarity > (variance_dissimilarity_mean + 2 * variance_dissimilarity_sd) ~ "Variance Outlier",
      TRUE ~ "None"
    )
  )

# Create the scatter plot with mean dissimilarity vs. variance
scatter_plot <- ggplot(dissimilarity_stats, aes(x = mean_dissimilarity, y = variance_dissimilarity, color = outlier_status)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3) + # Points, color based on outlier status
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Both Outliers" = "blue", "Variance Outlier" = "red", "Mean Outlier" = "black", "None" = "black"),
                     name = "Outlier Status",
                     breaks = c("Both Outliers", "Variance Outlier", "Mean Outlier"),
                     labels = c("Outlier in Both", "Outlier in Variance", "Outlier in Mean")) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Mean Dissimilarity Rating", limits = c(0, 7), breaks = seq(0, 7, by = 1), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Variance of Dissimilarity Ratings", limits = c(0, max(dissimilarity_stats$variance_dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5), breaks = seq(0, max(dissimilarity_stats$variance_dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE), by = 1), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))) +
  labs(title = paste("(n =", n.participants(data), ")")) +
  theme_pubr()+
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "plain", size = 20),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain", vjust = -0.1),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain", margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0), hjust = 0.65),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain", colour = "black"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain", colour = "black"),
      axis.line = element_line(size = 1.5),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "white"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none"
    ) 
  # Add participant labels showing the first 6 digits of the participant ID for outliers
  # geom_text_repel(
  #   data = dissimilarity_stats %>% filter(outlier_status != "None"),
  #   aes(label = substr(participant, 1, 6)),
  #   size = 3,
  #   color = "black",
  #   box.padding = 0.2,
  #   point.padding = 0.3,
  #   segment.size = 0.3
  # )

# Add marginal histograms using ggExtra
scatter_with_marginals <- ggMarginal(
  scatter_plot,
  type = "histogram",
  margins = "both",
  size = 5,
  fill = "gray",
  xparams = list(binwidth = 0.5, color = "black"),
  yparams = list(binwidth = 1, color = "black")
)

# Print the combined plot with marginal histograms
print(scatter_with_marginals)

# Save the combined plot as a PNG file
ggsave("Mean_vs_Variance_Dissimilarity_Scatter_With_Histograms.png", plot = scatter_with_marginals, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

# Generate scatterplots for each outlier participant
outlier_participants <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
  filter(outlier_status != "None") %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    plot = plot_individual_scatter(participant, outlier_status)
  )

```
##7. Final removal of outlier participants 
```{r, outlier removal}


# Step 1: Identify participants who are variance outliers based on the previous calculations
variance_outliers <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
  filter(outlier_status == "Variance Outlier" | outlier_status == "Both Outliers") %>%
  pull(participant)

# Step 2: Filter out variance outliers from 'trialdata' and add them to 'lowdpdata'
trialdata <- trialdata %>%
  filter(!participant %in% variance_outliers)

# Add variance outliers to 'lowdpdata'
lowdpdata <- bind_rows(lowdpdata, data %>% filter(participant %in% variance_outliers))

# Step 3: Count the number of participants in each dataset
total_participants <- n.participants(data)
trialdata_participants <- n.participants(trialdata)
lowdpdata_participants <- n.participants(lowdpdata)

# Print the number of participants across the datasets
cat("Total Participants:", total_participants, "\n")
cat("Participants in Trial Data:", trialdata_participants, "\n")
cat("Participants in Low DP Data:", lowdpdata_participants, "\n")
 
# Check consistency
if (trialdata_participants + lowdpdata_participants == total_participants) {
  cat("All participants are correctly allocated between 'trialdata' and 'lowdpdata'.\n")
} else {
  cat("Mismatch in participant allocation. Please check the filtering steps.\n")
}
```

## 8. Swap ordering in trialdata
```{r, swap}
# Needs to be done before computing statistics 


trialdata <- trialdata %>%
  # 1) Number trials within each participant
  group_by(participant) %>%
  mutate(trial_rank = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  # 2) For trials >150, swap circle1↔circle2
  mutate(
    tmp_SF   = circle1_SF,
    tmp_ori  = circle1_ori,
    circle1_SF  = ifelse(trial_rank > 150, circle2_SF, circle1_SF),
    circle1_ori = ifelse(trial_rank > 150, circle2_ori, circle1_ori),
    circle2_SF  = ifelse(trial_rank > 150, tmp_SF, circle2_SF),
    circle2_ori = ifelse(trial_rank > 150, tmp_ori, circle2_ori)
  ) %>%
  
  # 3) Clean up
  select(-tmp_SF, -tmp_ori, -trial_rank)
```
## ______________For distributed  main trials_____________________



##DISTRIBUTED Pass Check + Count

```{r check for duplicate conditions} 
### This code is a part of the distributed trial paradigm we follow 
### It first checks to make sure there are no duplicate data sets, 
### This was a problem in our early days of developing this method
### It is largely fixed now, so it may not do anything, however you must run it 
### to set up the right data frames for further analysis  

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)

spatial_freqs <- sort(unique(trialdata$circle1_SF))
num_spatial_freqs <- length(spatial_freqs)

# Group the data by participant and stimulus conditions, then count the occurrences
duplicate_data <- data_for_count %>%
  group_by(participant, circle1_ori, circle2_ori, circle1_SF, circle2_SF) %>%
  summarise(occurrences = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  filter(occurrences > 1)
print(duplicate_data)

# Check timestamps (if needed)
data_time_check <- pretimedata %>%
  select(participant, date, circle1_ori, circle2_ori, circle1_SF, circle2_SF) %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  mutate(trial_rank = row_number()) %>%
  filter(trial_rank <= 150) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-trial_rank) %>%
  arrange(circle1_ori, circle2_ori, circle1_SF, circle2_SF)

### Counting matrix and histogram

# Step 1: Split trialdata into two separate blocks (first 150 vs. next 150 trials per participant)
block1 <- trialdata %>% group_by(participant) %>% slice(1:150) %>% ungroup()
block2 <- trialdata %>% group_by(participant) %>% slice(151:300) %>% ungroup()

# For second block, swap circle1 and circle2 columns to reflect reversed presentation order
block2 <- block2 %>%
  mutate(
    tmp_SF  = circle1_SF,
    tmp_ori = circle1_ori,
    circle1_SF  = circle2_SF,
    circle1_ori = circle2_ori,
    circle2_SF  = tmp_SF,
    circle2_ori = tmp_ori
  ) %>%
  select(-tmp_SF, -tmp_ori)

# Step 2: Add combined keys for SF_Ori to each block
df_keys <- function(df) {
  df %>% mutate(
    circle1_key = paste(circle1_SF, circle1_ori, sep = "_"),
    circle2_key = paste(circle2_SF, circle2_ori, sep = "_")
  )
}
b1 <- df_keys(block1)
b2 <- df_keys(block2)

# Step 3: Count observed combinations in each block
counts1 <- b1 %>%
  group_by(circle1_key, circle2_key) %>%
  summarize(count = n(), .groups = "drop")
counts2 <- b2 %>%
  group_by(circle1_key, circle2_key) %>%
  summarize(count = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Step 4: Generate the full set of 48 stimulus keys
all_keys <- expand.grid(
  SF  = c(2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64),
  Ori = c(0, 45, 90, 135, 180, 225, 270, 315)
) %>%
  arrange(SF, Ori) %>%
  mutate(key = paste(SF, Ori, sep = "_")) %>%
  pull(key)

# Step 5: Ensure every possible combination appears (fill missing with zero count)
counts1 <- counts1 %>% complete(circle1_key = all_keys, circle2_key = all_keys, fill = list(count = 0))
counts2 <- counts2 %>% complete(circle1_key = all_keys, circle2_key = all_keys, fill = list(count = 0))

# Step 6: Map keys to numeric indices for heatmap axes
key_index <- setNames(seq_along(all_keys), all_keys)
counts1 <- counts1 %>% mutate(circle1_index = key_index[circle1_key], circle2_index = key_index[circle2_key])
counts2 <- counts2 %>% mutate(circle1_index = key_index[circle1_key], circle2_index = key_index[circle2_key])

# Step 7: Combine counts from both blocks into a single dataset
counts_combined <- counts1 %>%
  rename(count1 = count) %>%
  left_join(counts2 %>% rename(count2 = count), by = c("circle1_key", "circle2_key", "circle1_index", "circle2_index")) %>%
  mutate(count = count1 + count2) %>%
  select(circle1_key, circle2_key, circle1_index, circle2_index, count)

# Common plot settings
breaks <- seq_along(all_keys)
labels <- all_keys

draw_matrix <- function(data, title_text) {
  ggplot(data, aes(x = circle1_index, y = circle2_index, fill = count)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "black", high = "white", name = " Count",
#      breaks = 12:38, labels = 12:38,
      guide = guide_colourbar(barwidth = unit(0.4, "in") * 3,
                              barheight = unit(2.2, "in") * 3)) +
    labs(x = "Stimulus 1 (SF_Orientation)", y = "Stimulus 2 (SF_Orientation)", title = title_text) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
      axis.title    = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text.x   = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 8),
      legend.title  = element_text(size = 20),
      legend.text   = element_text(size = 20),
      panel.grid    = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_blank()
    ) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = seq(0.5, 48.5, by = 8), color = "white") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = seq(0.5, 48.5, by = 8), color = "white") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks, labels = labels, limits = c(0.5, 48.5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks, labels = labels, limits = c(0.5, 48.5))
}

# Step 8: Plot matrices for each block and the combined data
p_countdata1 <- draw_matrix(counts1, "First Block Count Matrix")
p_countdata2 <- draw_matrix(counts2, "Second Block Count Matrix")
p_countdata_combined <- draw_matrix(counts_combined, "Combined Count Matrix")

# Step 9: Plot the histogram for block 1 (unchanged)
p_hist <- ggplot(counts_combined, aes(x = count)) + 
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, color = "black", fill = "blue") +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "Count",
       y = "Number of Unique Combinations") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 30),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 30, margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.line = element_line(size = 1.5),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(10, 50), breaks = seq(10, 50, by = 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, 500), breaks = seq(0, 500, by = 50))

# Step 10: Save and print all plots
ggsave("countdata_block1.png", plot = p_countdata1, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("countdata_block2.png", plot = p_countdata2, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("countdata_combined.png", plot = p_countdata_combined, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("counthist.png", plot = p_hist, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

print(p_countdata1)
print(p_countdata2)
print(p_countdata_combined)
print(p_hist)


# Split into first and second blocks
block1 <- trialdata %>% group_by(participant) %>% slice(1:150) %>% ungroup()
block2 <- trialdata %>% group_by(participant) %>% slice(151:300) %>% ungroup() %>%
  # Swap for reversed presentation
  mutate(
    tmp_SF  = circle1_SF,
    tmp_ori = circle1_ori,
    circle1_SF  = circle2_SF,
    circle1_ori = circle2_ori,
    circle2_SF  = tmp_SF,
    circle2_ori = tmp_ori
  ) %>% select(-tmp_SF, -tmp_ori)

# Helper to compute dissimilarity matrix for a block
compute_dissimilarity_plot <- function(df, title_suffix) {
  # Aggregate mean dissimilarities
  agg <- df %>%
    mutate(
      circle1_key = paste(circle1_SF, circle1_ori, sep = "_"),
      circle2_key = paste(circle2_SF, circle2_ori, sep = "_")) %>%
    group_by(circle1_key, circle2_key) %>%
    summarize(mean_dissimilarity = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop')
  # Separate keys back to numeric
  agg <- agg %>%
    separate(circle1_key, into = c("circle1_SF","circle1_ori"), sep = "_", convert = TRUE) %>%
    separate(circle2_key, into = c("circle2_SF","circle2_ori"), sep = "_", convert = TRUE) %>%
    arrange(circle1_SF, circle2_SF, circle1_ori, circle2_ori) %>%
    # Recreate keys and mapping
    mutate(
      circle1_key = paste(circle1_SF, circle1_ori, sep = "_"),
      circle2_key = paste(circle2_SF, circle2_ori, sep = "_")
    )
  unique_keys <- unique(c(agg$circle1_key, agg$circle2_key))
  key_index <- setNames(seq_along(unique_keys), unique_keys)
  mapped <- agg %>% mutate(
    circle1_index = key_index[circle1_key],
    circle2_index = key_index[circle2_key]
  )

  # Orientation & SF labels for axes\ n  spatial_freqs <- sort(unique(df$circle1_SF))
  num_spatial_freqs <- length(spatial_freqs)
  num_combinations_per_frequency <- 48 / num_spatial_freqs
  breaks <- seq(num_combinations_per_frequency/2, 48, by = num_combinations_per_frequency)
  grid_breaks <- seq(0.5, 48+0.5, by = num_combinations_per_frequency)
  labels <- as.character(spatial_freqs)

  # Create wide matrix
  wide_df <- mapped %>% select(circle1_key, circle2_key, mean_dissimilarity) %>%
    spread(key = circle2_key, value = mean_dissimilarity)
  mat <- as.matrix(wide_df[-1])
  rownames(mat) <- wide_df$circle1_key
  # Sort names
  sort_names <- function(nms) {
    parts <- do.call(rbind, strsplit(nms, "_")) |> as.data.frame()
    names(parts) <- c("SF","Ori")
    parts <- mutate_all(parts, as.numeric)
    ordered <- parts %>% arrange(SF, Ori)
    paste(ordered$SF, ordered$Ori, sep = "_")
  }
  ordered_rows <- sort_names(rownames(mat))
  ordered_cols <- sort_names(colnames(mat))
  mat <- mat[ordered_rows, ordered_cols]

  # Melt back to long
  df_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(mat))
  names(df_long) <- c("circle1_key","circle2_key","mean_dissimilarity")

  # Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = circle1_key, y = circle2_key, fill = mean_dissimilarity)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "black", na.value = "red", name = "Dissimilarity",
      breaks = 0:7, labels = 0:7, limits = c(0,7),
      guide = guide_colourbar(barwidth = unit(0.4,"in")*3, barheight = unit(2.4,"in")*3)) +
    labs(x = "First Stimulus", y = "Second Stimulus",
         title = sprintf("Dissimilarity by SF for n = %d (%s)",
                         length(unique(df$participant)), title_suffix)) +
    theme_minimal() + theme(
      plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 20),
      axis.title   = element_text(size = 14),
      axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.text  = element_text(size = 16),
      panel.grid   = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_blank()
    ) + coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white") +
    scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_rows) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = ordered_cols)
  return(p)
}

# Generate two dissimilarity plots
p_diss1 <- compute_dissimilarity_plot(block1, "Block 1")
p_diss2 <- compute_dissimilarity_plot(block2, "Block 2")
# Save and print
ggsave("diss_block1.png", plot = p_diss1, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("diss_block2.png", plot = p_diss2, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
print(p_diss1)
print(p_diss2)



# Build a long dataframe of counts for each “block”
hist_df <- bind_rows(
  counts1 %>% mutate(block = "Block 1"),
  counts2 %>% mutate(block = "Block 2"),
  counts_combined %>% mutate(block = "Combined")
)

# Faceted histograms
p_triple <- ggplot(hist_df, aes(x = count)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, color = "black", fill = "blue") +
  facet_wrap(~block, nrow = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Count Distributions by Block",
    x = "Count",
    y = "Number of Stimulus Pairs"
  ) +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    strip.text   = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title   = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text    = element_text(size = 10)
  )
ggsave("count_triple.png", plot = p_triple, width = 20, height = 10, dpi = 300)


stats_counts <- counts_combined %>%
  summarise(
    mean_count = mean(count),
    var_count  = var(count),
    med_count = median(count),
    sd_count   = sd(count)
  )

print(stats_counts)


```


## DISTRIBUTED Reaction Time 
```{r,reaction time}
# Load the necessary library
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)

trialdata$trial <- rep(1:300, times = length(unique(trialdata$participant)))

# Ensure the 'participant' column is treated as a factor for distinct lines for each participant
trialdata$participant <- as.factor(trialdata$participant)

summary(trialdata$response_time)


# Create the line plot
p <- ggplot(trialdata, aes(x = trial, y = response_time, color = participant, group = participant)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Participant Response Times Over the Course of the Experiment",
       x = "Observation Number",
       y = "Response Time",
       color = "Participant") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave("responsetime.png", plot = p, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)


p <- ggplot(trialdata, aes(x = trial, y = response_time, color = participant, group = participant)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_log10() +  # Apply a logarithmic scale to the y-axis
  labs(title = "Participant Response Times Over 300 Trials (Log Scale)",
       x = "Trial Number",
       y = "Response Time (Log Scale)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")  # Hides the legend


ggsave("responsetimelog.png", plot = p, width = 20, height = 20, dpi = 300)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = participant, y = response_time, fill = participant)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Response Times Across Participants",
       x = "Participant",
       y = "Response Time") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")  # Hides the legend

ggsave("responsetimeviolin.png", plot = p, width = 20, height = 20, dpi = 300)

```

##DISTRIBUTED Matrix Direction x SF
     Run this to get: 
        Raw matrix 
        Sym matrix 

```{r, Distributed matrix Ori X SF}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# Step 1: Aggregate data
trialdata_aggregated <- trialdata %>%
  mutate(circle1_key = paste(circle1_ori, circle1_SF, sep = "_"),
         circle2_key = paste(circle2_ori, circle2_SF, sep = "_")) %>%
  group_by(circle1_key, circle2_key) %>%
  summarize(mean_dissimilarity = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  separate(circle1_key, into = c("circle1_ori", "circle1_SF"), sep = "_") %>%
  separate(circle2_key, into = c("circle2_ori", "circle2_SF"), sep = "_") %>%
  mutate(across(c(circle1_ori, circle2_ori, circle1_SF, circle2_SF), as.numeric)) %>%
  arrange(circle1_ori, circle2_ori, circle1_SF, circle2_SF)


# Index mapping
trialdata_aggregated <- trialdata_aggregated %>%
  mutate(circle1_key = paste(circle1_ori, circle1_SF, sep = "_"),
         circle2_key = paste(circle2_ori, circle2_SF, sep = "_"))

unique_keys <- unique(c(trialdata_aggregated$circle1_key, trialdata_aggregated$circle2_key))
key_index <- setNames(seq_along(unique_keys), unique_keys)

# Map keys to indices, respecting the new order
trialdata_mapped <- trialdata_aggregated %>%
  mutate(circle1_index = key_index[circle1_key],
         circle2_index = key_index[circle2_key])

# Step 2: Plotting

# Assuming a vector of orientations and spatial frequencies
orientations <- sort(unique(trialdata$circle1_ori))
num_orientations <- length(orientations)

# If every orientation is equally represented across spatial frequencies
# and assuming a total of 48 unique combinations (8 orientations * 6 spatial frequencies)
num_combinations_per_orientation <- 48 / num_orientations

# Generate breaks at the midpoint of each orientation's range
breaks <- seq(num_combinations_per_orientation / 2, 48, by = num_combinations_per_orientation)

# Calculate breaks for grid lines at the end of each orientation group
grid_breaks <- seq(0.5, 48 + 0.5, by = num_combinations_per_orientation)

# Assuming orientations are your labels but check if this aligns with your indexing
labels <- as.character(orientations)

# Ensure the number of breaks matches the number of labels
if(length(breaks) != length(labels)) {
  warning("The number of breaks does not match the number of labels. Adjusting...")
  # Adjust either breaks or labels here to match.
}

write.csv(trialdata_mapped, "trialdata_mapped.csv", row.names = FALSE)


# Plotting

#dissimilarity Matrix
p_distdata <- ggplot(trialdata_mapped, aes(x = circle1_index, y = circle2_index, fill = mean_dissimilarity)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "black", na.value = "red", name = "dissimilarity",
                      breaks = 0:7,  # Specify breaks to include all values from 1 to 8
                      labels = 0:7,
                      guide = guide_colourbar(barwidth = unit(0.2, "in") * 3, 
                                              barheight = unit(1, "in") * 3)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "", title = sprintf("dissimilarity Matrix by Orientation for n = %d", n.participants(trialdata))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    #Plot Title
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "plain", size = 20),
    
    #Axis titles
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "plain"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "plain"),
    
    #Axis text
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "plain"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "plain"),
    
    #Gridlines
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),  # Remove major gridlines
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),  # Remove minor gridlines
    panel.background = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove panel background
    #panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill=NA)  # Optional: Add border around the plot
  ) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white", linetype = "solid") +  # Add vertical grid lines
  geom_hline(yintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white", linetype = "solid") +  # Add horizontal grid lines
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks, labels = labels, limits = c(0.5, 48.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks, labels = labels, limits = c(0.5, 48.5))

#####################
#####Symmetric matrix 
#####################

# First, create a wide table where each cell contains the mean_dissimilarity for each key combination
dissimilarity_df <- trialdata_mapped %>%
  dplyr::select(circle1_key, circle2_key, mean_dissimilarity) %>%
  unique() %>%
  spread(key = circle2_key, value = mean_dissimilarity)

# Convert to matrix
dissimilarity_matrix <- as.matrix(dissimilarity_df[-1])
rownames(dissimilarity_matrix) <- dissimilarity_df$circle1_key

matrix_data <- dissimilarity_matrix
  
# Function to sort the names based on your criteria
sort_names <- function(names) {
  # Split names into two parts based on underscore
  parts <- strsplit(names, "_")
  # Convert parts into a data frame
  parts_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(parts, function(x) as.numeric(x)))
  colnames(parts_df) <- c("part1", "part2")
  # Convert to data frame for easier manipulation
  parts_df <- as.data.frame(parts_df)
  # Arrange by the first part, then by the specific sequence for the second part
  ordered_df <- parts_df %>%
    arrange(part1, match(part2, c(2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64)))
  # Combine back into the original format
  ordered_names <- paste(ordered_df$part1, ordered_df$part2, sep = "_")
  return(ordered_names)
}

# Apply sorting function to row and column names
ordered_row_names <- sort_names(rownames(matrix_data))
ordered_col_names <- sort_names(colnames(matrix_data))

# Reorder the matrix
dissimilarity_matrix <- matrix_data[ordered_row_names, ordered_col_names]

# Symmetrize the dissimilarity matrix
sym_dissimilarity_matrix <- (dissimilarity_matrix + t(dissimilarity_matrix)) / 2
sym_dissimilarity_matrix[is.na(sym_dissimilarity_matrix)] <- 0  # Setting NA values to 0

# Plotting the symmetrized dissimilarity matrix
# Convert the symmetrized matrix back to a long format for ggplot
sym_dissimilarity_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(sym_dissimilarity_matrix))
colnames(sym_dissimilarity_df) <- c("circle1_key", "circle2_key", "mean_dissimilarity")

# Create the plot
p_sym_Ori_x_SF <- ggplot(sym_dissimilarity_df, aes(x = circle1_key, y = circle2_key, fill = mean_dissimilarity)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "black", na.value = "red", name = "dissimilarity",
                      breaks = 0:7,  # Adjust these breaks based on your actual data
                      labels = 0:7,
                      guide = guide_colourbar(barwidth = unit(0.2, "in") * 3, 
                                              barheight = unit(1, "in") * 3)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "", title = sprintf("dissimilarity Matrix by Direction for n = %d", n.participants(trialdata))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "plain", size = 20),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "plain"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "plain"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white", linetype = "solid") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white", linetype = "solid") +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_row_names) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = ordered_col_names)

ggsave("Sym_distdata_Ori_x_SF.png", plot = p_sym_Ori_x_SF, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
p_sym_Ori_x_SF

```


##DISTRIBUTED SF x Direction + MDS + Diag
Run this to get: 
        Raw matrix 
        Sym matrix 
        MDS with icon arrows
        Diagonal Analysis

```{r, Distributed matrix SF x Ori}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggimage)
library(ggdendro)
library(reshape2)
library(dendextend)
library(patchwork)

# Step 1: Aggregate data to swap it into SF x Ori
trialdata_aggregated <- trialdata %>%
  mutate(circle1_key = paste(circle1_SF, circle1_ori, sep = "_"),
         circle2_key = paste(circle2_SF, circle2_ori, sep = "_")) %>%
  group_by(circle1_key, circle2_key) %>%
  summarize(mean_dissimilarity = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  separate(circle1_key, into = c("circle1_SF", "circle1_ori"), sep = "_") %>%
  separate(circle2_key, into = c("circle2_SF", "circle2_ori"), sep = "_") %>%
  mutate(across(c(circle1_SF, circle2_SF, circle1_ori, circle2_ori), as.numeric)) %>%
  arrange(circle1_SF, circle2_SF, circle1_ori, circle2_ori)

# Index mapping
trialdata_aggregated <- trialdata_aggregated %>%
  mutate(circle1_key = paste(circle1_SF, circle1_ori, sep = "_"),
         circle2_key = paste(circle2_SF, circle2_ori, sep = "_"))

unique_keys <- unique(c(trialdata_aggregated$circle1_key, trialdata_aggregated$circle2_key))
key_index <- setNames(seq_along(unique_keys), unique_keys)

# Map keys to indices, respecting the new order
trialdata_mapped <- trialdata_aggregated %>%
  mutate(circle1_index = key_index[circle1_key],
         circle2_index = key_index[circle2_key])

# Step 2: Plotting

# Assuming a vector of orientations and spatial frequencies
spatial_freqs <- sort(unique(trialdata$circle1_SF))
num_spatial_freqs <- length(spatial_freqs)

# If every orientation is equally represented across spatial frequencies
# and assuming a total of 48 unique combinations (8 orientations * 6 spatial frequencies)
num_combinations_per_frequency <- 48 / num_spatial_freqs

# Generate breaks at the midpoint of each orientation's range
breaks <- seq(num_combinations_per_frequency / 2, 48, by = num_combinations_per_frequency)

# Calculate breaks for grid lines at the end of each frequency group
grid_breaks <- seq(0.5, 48 + 0.5, by = num_combinations_per_frequency)

# Assuming frequencies are your labels but check if this aligns with your indexing
labels <- as.character(spatial_freqs)

# Ensure the number of breaks matches the number of labels
if(length(breaks) != length(labels)) {
  warning("The number of breaks does not match the number of labels. Adjusting...")
  # Adjust either breaks or labels here to match.
}

#####################
##### Non-Symmetric matrix 
#####################

# First, create a wide table where each cell contains the mean_dissimilarity for each key combination
dissimilarity_df <- trialdata_mapped %>%
  dplyr::select(circle1_key, circle2_key, mean_dissimilarity) %>%
  unique() %>%
  spread(key = circle2_key, value = mean_dissimilarity)

# Convert to matrix
dissimilarity_matrix <- as.matrix(dissimilarity_df[-1])
rownames(dissimilarity_matrix) <- dissimilarity_df$circle1_key

matrix_data <- dissimilarity_matrix
  
# Function to sort the names based on your criteria
sort_names <- function(names) {
  # Split names into two parts based on underscore
  parts <- strsplit(names, "_")
  # Convert parts into a data frame
  parts_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(parts, function(x) as.numeric(x)))
  colnames(parts_df) <- c("SF", "Ori")
  # Convert to data frame for easier manipulation
  parts_df <- as.data.frame(parts_df)
  # Arrange by SF and then Ori
  ordered_df <- parts_df %>%
    arrange(SF, Ori)
  # Combine back into the original format
  ordered_names <- paste(ordered_df$SF, ordered_df$Ori, sep = "_")
  return(ordered_names)
}


# Apply sorting function to row and column names
ordered_row_names <- sort_names(rownames(matrix_data))
ordered_col_names <- sort_names(colnames(matrix_data))

# Reorder the matrix
dissimilarity_matrix <- matrix_data[ordered_row_names, ordered_col_names]

print(rownames(dissimilarity_matrix))

# Convert the symmetrised matrix back to a long format for ggplot
dissimilarity_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(dissimilarity_matrix))
colnames(dissimilarity_df) <- c("circle1_key", "circle2_key", "mean_dissimilarity")

# Create the plot
p_raw_SF_x_Ori <- ggplot(dissimilarity_df, aes(x = circle1_key, y = circle2_key, fill = mean_dissimilarity)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "black", na.value = "red", name = "Dissimilarity",
                      breaks = 0:7,  # Adjust these breaks based on your actual data
                      labels = 0:7,
                                            limits = c(0,7),
                      guide = guide_colourbar(barwidth = unit(0.4, "in") * 3, 
                                              barheight = unit(2.4, "in") * 3)) +
  labs(x = "First Stimulus", y = "Second Stimulus", title = sprintf("Disssimilarity by SF for n = %d", trialdata_participants, "\n")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "plain", size = 20),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "plain"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "plain"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white", linetype = "solid") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white", linetype = "solid") +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_row_names) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = ordered_col_names)

ggsave("raw_distdata_SF_x_Ori.png", plot = p_raw_SF_x_Ori, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
p_raw_SF_x_Ori

#### Raw matrix diagonals 

# Function to calculate mean and SD for each diagonal (offset)
mean_diag_dissimilarity <- function(matrix) {
  max_dim <- dim(matrix)[1]  # assuming the matrix is square
  
  diag_stats <- lapply(0:(max_dim - 1), function(k) {
    if (k == 0) {
      # Main diagonal
      diag_elements <- matrix[row(matrix) == col(matrix)]
    } else {
      # Combine both upper and lower diagonals for offset k
      diag_elements <- c(
        matrix[row(matrix) == (col(matrix) - k)],
        matrix[row(matrix) == (col(matrix) + k)]
      )
    }
    data.frame(
      Diagonal = k,
      MeanDissimilarity = mean(diag_elements, na.rm = TRUE),
      SD = sd(diag_elements, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  })
  
  diag_stats_df <- do.call(rbind, diag_stats)
  return(diag_stats_df)
}


diagonals_df <- mean_diag_dissimilarity(dissimilarity_matrix)

# Assign groups based on diagonal offset
diagonals_df$Group <- "Mixed"
diagonals_df$Group[seq(5, nrow(diagonals_df), 8)] <- "Opposite"
diagonals_df$Group[seq(9, nrow(diagonals_df), 8)] <- "Same"
# Force the first row (offset = 0) to be "Same"
diagonals_df$Group[1] <- "Same"

# Create subsets for each group to draw separate trend lines
subset_mixed <- subset(diagonals_df, Group == 'Mixed')
subset_opp   <- subset(diagonals_df, Group == 'Opposite')
subset_same  <- subset(diagonals_df, Group == 'Same')

# Fit linear regression models for each group
model1 <- lm(MeanDissimilarity ~ Diagonal, data = subset_opp)
model2 <- lm(MeanDissimilarity ~ Diagonal, data = subset_same)
model3 <- lm(MeanDissimilarity ~ Diagonal, data = subset_mixed)

# Print model summaries
print("Summary for Opposite group (e.g., 180°):")
print(summary(model1))
print("Summary for Same group (e.g., 0°):")
print(summary(model2))
print("Summary for Mixed group:")
print(summary(model3))

# Perform ANOVA across groups
anova_model <- aov(MeanDissimilarity ~ Group, data = diagonals_df)
print("ANOVA Summary:")
print(summary(anova_model))

# Tukey's HSD test for multiple comparisons
tukey_test <- TukeyHSD(anova_model)
print("Tukey HSD Results:")
print(tukey_test)

# Extract and print p-values
tukey_p_values <- tukey_test$Group[, "p adj"]
names(tukey_p_values) <- rownames(tukey_test$Group)
print(tukey_p_values)

# Create the scatter plot with error bars and trend lines
coloured <- ggplot(diagonals_df, aes(x = Diagonal, y = MeanDissimilarity)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Group), size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(color = Group, ymin = MeanDissimilarity - SD, ymax = MeanDissimilarity + SD), width = 0.2) +
  geom_smooth(data = subset_opp, aes(color = Group), method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  geom_smooth(data = subset_same, aes(color = Group), method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  geom_smooth(data = subset_mixed, aes(color = Group), method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "± Offset from Primary Diagonal",
       y = "Mean Dissimilarity") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "plain", size = 20),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 45, face = "plain"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 50, face = "plain"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 40, face = "plain"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 40, face = "plain", colour = "black"),
    axis.line    = element_line(size = 1.5),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "none"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 7), breaks = seq(0, 7, by = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(c(seq(0, 40, by = 10), 48)))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Opposite" = "red", "Same" = "blue", "Mixed" = "purple"))

# Save and display the scatter plot
ggsave("coloured_mean_dissimilarity.png", plot = coloured, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
print(coloured)

#####################
##### Symmetric matrix 
#####################

# First, create a wide table where each cell contains the mean_dissimilarity for each key combination
dissimilarity_df <- trialdata_mapped %>%
  dplyr::select(circle1_key, circle2_key, mean_dissimilarity) %>%
  unique() %>%
  spread(key = circle2_key, value = mean_dissimilarity)

# Convert to matrix
dissimilarity_matrix <- as.matrix(dissimilarity_df[-1])
rownames(dissimilarity_matrix) <- dissimilarity_df$circle1_key

matrix_data <- dissimilarity_matrix
  
# Function to sort the names based on your criteria
sort_names <- function(names) {
  # Split names into two parts based on underscore
  parts <- strsplit(names, "_")
  # Convert parts into a data frame
  parts_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(parts, function(x) as.numeric(x)))
  colnames(parts_df) <- c("SF", "Ori")
  # Convert to data frame for easier manipulation
  parts_df <- as.data.frame(parts_df)
  # Arrange by SF and then Ori
  ordered_df <- parts_df %>%
    arrange(SF, Ori)
  # Combine back into the original format
  ordered_names <- paste(ordered_df$SF, ordered_df$Ori, sep = "_")
  return(ordered_names)
}


# Apply sorting function to row and column names
ordered_row_names <- sort_names(rownames(matrix_data))
ordered_col_names <- sort_names(colnames(matrix_data))

# Reorder the matrix
dissimilarity_matrix <- matrix_data[ordered_row_names, ordered_col_names]

print(rownames(dissimilarity_matrix))

# Symmetrize the dissimilarity matrix
sym_dissimilarity_matrix <- (dissimilarity_matrix + t(dissimilarity_matrix)) / 2

rownames(sym_dissimilarity_matrix) <- ordered_row_names
colnames(sym_dissimilarity_matrix) <- ordered_row_names

print(rownames(sym_dissimilarity_matrix))

write.csv(sym_dissimilarity_matrix, "sym_dissimilarity_matrix.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Plotting the symmetrised dissimilarity matrix

# Convert the symmetrised matrix back to a long format for ggplot
sym_dissimilarity_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(sym_dissimilarity_matrix))
colnames(sym_dissimilarity_df) <- c("circle1_key", "circle2_key", "mean_dissimilarity")

# Create the plot
p_sym_SF_x_Ori <- ggplot(sym_dissimilarity_df, aes(x = circle1_key, y = circle2_key, fill = mean_dissimilarity)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "black", na.value = "red", name = "Dissimilarity",
                      breaks = 0:7,  # Adjust these breaks based on your actual data
                      labels = 0:7,
                                            limits = c(0,7),
                      guide = guide_colourbar(barwidth = unit(0.4, "in") * 3, 
                                              barheight = unit(2.4, "in") * 3)) +
  labs(x = "First Stimulus", y = "Second Stimulus", title = sprintf("Disssimilarity by SF for n = %d", trialdata_participants, "\n")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "plain", size = 20),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "plain"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "plain"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white", linetype = "solid") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = grid_breaks, color = "white", linetype = "solid") +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_row_names) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = ordered_col_names)

ggsave("Sym_distdata_SF_x_Ori.png", plot = p_sym_SF_x_Ori, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
p_sym_SF_x_Ori


####################
### MDS w/ ICONS ###
####################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggimage)
library(smacof)

# First, create a wide table where each cell contains the mean_dissimilarity for each key combination
dissimilarity_df <- trialdata_mapped %>%
  dplyr::select(circle1_key, circle2_key, mean_dissimilarity) %>%
  unique() %>%
  spread(key = circle2_key, value = mean_dissimilarity)

# Convert to matrix
dissimilarity_matrix <- as.matrix(dissimilarity_df[-1])
rownames(dissimilarity_matrix) <- dissimilarity_df$circle1_key

matrix_data <- dissimilarity_matrix

# Function to sort the names based on your criteria
sort_names <- function(names) {
  parts <- strsplit(names, "_")
  parts_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(parts, function(x) as.numeric(x)))
  colnames(parts_df) <- c("SF", "Ori")
  parts_df <- as.data.frame(parts_df)
  ordered_df <- parts_df %>% arrange(SF, Ori)
  ordered_names <- paste(ordered_df$SF, ordered_df$Ori, sep = "_")
  return(ordered_names)
}

# Apply sorting function to row and column names
ordered_row_names <- sort_names(rownames(matrix_data))
ordered_col_names <- sort_names(colnames(matrix_data))

# Reorder the matrix
dissimilarity_matrix <- matrix_data[ordered_row_names, ordered_col_names]

# Symmetrize the dissimilarity matrix
sym_dissimilarity_matrix <- (dissimilarity_matrix + t(dissimilarity_matrix)) / 2
diag(sym_dissimilarity_matrix) <- 0
rownames(sym_dissimilarity_matrix) <- ordered_row_names
colnames(sym_dissimilarity_matrix) <- ordered_row_names
# Run non-metric MDS using smacof
dist_obj <- as.dist(sym_dissimilarity_matrix)
mds_result <- smacofSym(dist_obj, ndim = 2)

# Convert MDS result to a dataframe for plotting
mds_df <- as.data.frame(mds_result$conf)
names(mds_df) <- c("Dimension 1", "Dimension 2")
mds_df$key <- rownames(mds_df)
mds_df$image_path <- paste0("Arrow_icons/", mds_df$key, ".png")

# Function to create MDS plot with icons
create_modified_plot_with_icons <- function(mds_df, title) {
  ggplot(mds_df, aes(x = `Dimension 1`, y = `Dimension 2`)) +
    geom_blank() +
    coord_fixed() +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-0.9, 0, 0.9), labels = c("-0.9", "0", "0.9"), limits = c(-0.9, 0.9)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(-0.9,0, 0.9), labels = c("-0.9", "0", "0.9"), limits = c(-0.9, 0.9)) +
    ggtitle(title) +
    theme_pubr() +
    theme(
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 40, face = "plain"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 40, face = "plain"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 40, face = "plain"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 40, face = "plain"),
      axis.line = element_line(size = 1.1),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 14)
    ) +
   # geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", size = 0.25, color = "black") +
   # geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", size = 0.25, color = "black") +
    geom_image(aes(image = image_path), size = 0.08)
}

# Apply the function to your MDS dataframe and set the title
dist_MDS_plot_with_icons <- create_modified_plot_with_icons(mds_df, "")
ggsave("mds_plot_with_icons_SF_x_Ori.png", plot = dist_MDS_plot_with_icons, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

####

mds_result_for_eig <- cmdscale(sym_dissimilarity_matrix, k = 2, eig = TRUE)

# Print Eig for stats
eigenvalues <- mds_result_for_eig$eig

# Check the number of positive eigenvalues
num_positive_eigenvalues <- sum(eigenvalues > 0)
print(paste("Number of positive eigenvalues:", num_positive_eigenvalues))

# If there are at least 8 positive eigenvalues, compute the variance explained
if (num_positive_eigenvalues >= 2) {
    total_variance <- sum(eigenvalues[1:2])
    total_variance_all_positive <- sum(eigenvalues[eigenvalues > 0])
    proportion_variance_explained <- total_variance / total_variance_all_positive
    print(paste("Proportion of variance explained by the first 2 dimensions:", proportion_variance_explained))
} else {
    print("Less than 8 positive eigenvalues available.")
}

```

## DISTRIBUTED Matrix Stats
```{r, human stats}
# Load necessary library
library(dplyr)
library(dplyr)

dissimilarity_stats <- trialdata %>%
  # 1. Tag each stimulus
  mutate(
    circle1_key = paste(circle1_SF, circle1_ori, sep = "_"),
    circle2_key = paste(circle2_SF, circle2_ori, sep = "_")
  ) %>%
  # 2. Collapse multiple trials *within* each participant × stimulus-pair
  group_by(participant, circle1_key, circle2_key) %>%
  summarize(
    participant_mean = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # 3. Now collapse *across* participants for each stimulus-pair
  group_by(circle1_key, circle2_key) %>% 
  summarize(
    mean_dissimilarity = mean(participant_mean),                    # grand mean
    sd_dissimilarity   = sd(participant_mean),                      # SD across participants
    se_dissimilarity   = sd_dissimilarity / sqrt(n()),              # standard error of the mean
    n_observations     = n(),                                       # how many contributed
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(circle1_key, circle2_key)

print(dissimilarity_stats)


# (a) Mean and SD for SF 2 vs. SF 2
sf2_vs_sf2_stats <- trialdata %>%
  filter(circle1_SF == 2, circle2_SF == 2) %>%
  summarize(mean_dissimilarity = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_dissimilarity = sd(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE))
print(sf2_vs_sf2_stats)

# (b) Mean and SD for SF 2 vs. SF 64 
# Include both directions: circle1 from SF2 and circle2 from SF64, or vice versa.
sf2_vs_sf64_stats <- trialdata %>%
  filter((circle1_SF == 2 & circle2_SF == 64)) %>%
  summarize(mean_dissimilarity = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_dissimilarity = sd(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE))
print(sf2_vs_sf64_stats)



# Define the two stimulus combinations you want to compare:
pair_A <- list(circle1_SF = 2, circle1_ori = 135,
               circle2_SF = 8, circle2_ori = 90)
pair_B <- list(circle1_SF = 8, circle1_ori = 90,
               circle2_SF = 2, circle2_ori = 135)

# Helper: build the key strings to match your existing mutate scheme
make_key <- function(SF, ori) paste(SF, ori, sep = "_")
key_A <- make_key(pair_A$circle1_SF, pair_A$circle1_ori)
key_A2 <- make_key(pair_A$circle2_SF, pair_A$circle2_ori)

# if you want to compare the same ordering in both pairs, 
# otherwise swap circle1/2 arguments appropriately.
stimA1 <- paste(pair_A$circle1_SF, pair_A$circle1_ori, sep = "_")
stimA2 <- paste(pair_A$circle2_SF, pair_A$circle2_ori, sep = "_")
stimB1 <- paste(pair_B$circle1_SF, pair_B$circle1_ori, sep = "_")
stimB2 <- paste(pair_B$circle2_SF, pair_B$circle2_ori, sep = "_")

# 1) Compute per-participant mean dissimilarity for each pair
participant_means <- trialdata %>%
  mutate(
    circle1_key = paste(circle1_SF, circle1_ori, sep = "_"),
    circle2_key = paste(circle2_SF, circle2_ori, sep = "_"),
    pair_key = paste(circle1_key, circle2_key, sep = "_vs_")
  ) %>%
  filter(pair_key %in% c(
    paste(stimA1, stimA2, sep = "_vs_"),
    paste(stimB1, stimB2, sep = "_vs_")
  )) %>%
  group_by(participant, pair_key) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_dis = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 2) Spread into wide form so each participant is one row,
#    with their A and B means as separate columns
wide_means <- participant_means %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = pair_key,
    values_from = mean_dis
  ) %>%
  # optionally, drop any participants who didn’t rate both
  drop_na()

# 3) Run the paired t-test
tres <- t.test(
  wide_means[[ paste(stimA1, stimA2, sep = "_vs_") ]],
  wide_means[[ paste(stimB1, stimB2, sep = "_vs_") ]],
  paired = TRUE
)

print(tres)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# 1) define your SFs and the four opposite‐direction orientation pairs
sfs       <- c(2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64)
ori_pairs <- list(
  c(0,   180),
  c(45,  225),
  c(90,  270),
  c(135, 315)
)

# 2) initialize an empty results tibble
results <- tibble(
  SF         = integer(),
  ori1       = integer(),
  ori2       = integer(),
  t_stat     = double(),
  df         = double(),
  p_value    = double(),
  mean_diff  = double(),
  significant= logical()
)

# 3) loop over each SF and each opposite‐direction pair
for (s in sfs) {
  for (pr in ori_pairs) {
    o1 <- pr[1]; o2 <- pr[2]
    
    # build the two keys for “circle1→circle2” vs “swapped”
    key_A <- sprintf("%d_%d_vs_%d_%d", s, o1, s, o2)
    key_B <- sprintf("%d_%d_vs_%d_%d", s, o2, s, o1)
    
    # compute per‐participant mean dissimilarities for these two keys
    pm <- trialdata %>%
      mutate(
        circle1_key = paste(circle1_SF, circle1_ori, sep = "_"),
        circle2_key = paste(circle2_SF, circle2_ori, sep = "_"),
        pair_key    = paste(circle1_key, circle2_key, sep = "_vs_")
      ) %>%
      filter(pair_key %in% c(key_A, key_B)) %>%
      group_by(participant, pair_key) %>%
      summarize(mean_dis = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = pair_key, values_from = mean_dis) %>%
      drop_na()
    
    # only run t.test if everyone has both conditions
    if (nrow(pm) > 0) {
      tt <- t.test(pm[[key_A]], pm[[key_B]], paired = TRUE)
      results <- bind_rows(results,
        tibble(
          SF          = s,
          ori1        = o1,
          ori2        = o2,
          t_stat      = as.numeric(tt$statistic),
          df          = as.numeric(tt$parameter),
          p_value     = tt$p.value,
          mean_diff   = mean(pm[[key_A]] - pm[[key_B]]),
          significant = tt$p.value < 0.05
        )
      )
    } else {
      # no data for this pair
      results <- bind_rows(results,
        tibble(
          SF          = s,
          ori1        = o1,
          ori2        = o2,
          t_stat      = NA_real_,
          df          = NA_real_,
          p_value     = NA_real_,
          mean_diff   = NA_real_,
          significant = FALSE
        )
      )
    }
  }
}

# 4) inspect
print(results)

# show only the significant ones:
results %>% filter(significant)

```
## DISTRIBUTED Asymmetry
```{r, stats test}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)


### This version matches per participant to get a mean per participant for a particular trial order. Each participant contributes exactly one value for the A-order and one value for the B-order. It is not using “raw” trial-by-trial values—instead turning each participant’s potentially multiple A-trials into a single number, and likewise for B.

### this is a paired T test 

# 1) copy your data and add a `block` indicator based on trial order:
dissimilarity_stats <- trialdata %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  # assume trials 1–150 are block 1, 151–300 are block 2 for each participant
  mutate(block = if_else(row_number() <= 150, 1L, 2L)) %>%
  ungroup()

# 2) build keys for ordered vs swapped
dissimilarity_stats <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
  mutate(
    circle1_key   = paste(circle1_SF, circle1_ori, sep = "_"),
    circle2_key   = paste(circle2_SF, circle2_ori, sep = "_"),
    pair_key      = paste(circle1_key, circle2_key, sep = "_vs_"),
    unordered_pair = map2_chr(circle1_key, circle2_key,
                              ~ paste(sort(c(.x, .y)), collapse = "_vs_"))
  )

# 3) get the unique stimulus‐pairs (ignoring order)
unique_pairs <- unique(dissimilarity_stats$unordered_pair)

# 4) initialize results table
asym_results <- tibble(
  unordered_pair = character(),
  key_A          = character(),
  key_B          = character(),
  t_stat         = double(),
  df             = double(),
  p_value        = double(),
  mean_diff      = double(),
  significant    = logical()
)

# 5) loop over each unordered_pair
for (up in unique_pairs) {
  parts <- strsplit(up, "_vs_")[[1]]
  key_A <- paste(parts[1], parts[2], sep = "_vs_")
  key_B <- paste(parts[2], parts[1], sep = "_vs_")
  
  if (parts[1] == parts[2]) {
    # main‐diagonal: take the single block-1 trial and single block-2 trial
    dfA <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
      filter(pair_key == key_A, block == 1) %>%
      group_by(participant) %>%
      summarize(val_A = first(dissimilarity), .groups="drop")
    
    dfB <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
      filter(pair_key == key_B, block == 2) %>%
      group_by(participant) %>%
      summarize(val_B = first(dissimilarity), .groups="drop")
  } else {
    # off-diagonal: mean() is fine since there will only ever be one per block
    dfA <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
      filter(pair_key == key_A) %>%
      group_by(participant) %>%
      summarize(val_A = mean(dissimilarity), .groups="drop")
    
    dfB <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
      filter(pair_key == key_B) %>%
      group_by(participant) %>%
      summarize(val_B = mean(dissimilarity), .groups="drop")
  }
  
  dfAB <- inner_join(dfA, dfB, by="participant")
  
  if (nrow(dfAB) >= 2) {
    tt <- t.test(dfAB$val_A, dfAB$val_B, paired = TRUE)
    asym_results <- asym_results %>%
      add_row(
        unordered_pair = up,
        key_A          = key_A,
        key_B          = key_B,
        t_stat         = as.numeric(tt$statistic),
        df             = as.numeric(tt$parameter),
        p_value        = tt$p.value,
        mean_diff      = mean(dfAB$val_A - dfAB$val_B),
        significant    = tt$p.value < 0.05
      )
  } else {
    asym_results <- asym_results %>%
      add_row(
        unordered_pair = up,
        key_A          = key_A,
        key_B          = key_B,
        t_stat         = NA_real_,
        df             = NA_real_,
        p_value        = NA_real_,
        mean_diff      = NA_real_,
        significant    = FALSE
      )
  }
}

# 6) inspect your results
print(asym_results)

# 7) Build plot_df
plot_df <- asym_results %>%
  separate(unordered_pair, into = c("stim1", "stim2"), sep = "_vs_") %>%
  mutate(significant = p_value < 0.05)

# 8) Define axis ordering
sfs   <- c(2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64)
oris  <- c(0, 45, 90, 135, 180, 225, 270, 315)
stim_levels <- unlist(lapply(sfs, function(sf) paste(sf, oris, sep = "_")))

plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(
    stim1 = factor(stim1, levels = stim_levels),
    stim2 = factor(stim2, levels = stim_levels)
  )

# 9) Fold into lower triangle
tri_df <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(
    row = if_else(as.integer(stim2) > as.integer(stim1), stim2, stim1),
    col = if_else(as.integer(stim2) > as.integer(stim1), stim1, stim2),
    row = factor(row, levels = stim_levels),
    col = factor(col, levels = stim_levels)
  )

# 10) Heatmap of T‐statistics
p <- ggplot(tri_df, aes(x = col, y = row, fill = t_stat)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0,
    name     = "T-statistic"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data    = filter(tri_df, significant),
    aes(label = "*"),
    size    = 7,
    nudge_y = -0.3
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Stimulus 2 (SF_ori)",
    y = "Stimulus 1 (SF_ori)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid  = element_blank()
  ) +
  coord_fixed()

print(p)
ggsave("asymmetry_matrix.png", plot = p, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

# 11) Heatmap of mean differences
p2 <- ggplot(tri_df, aes(x = col, y = row, fill = mean_diff)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0,
    name     = "Mean Asymmetry"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Stimulus 2 (SF_ori)",
    y = "Stimulus 1 (SF_ori)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid  = element_blank()
  ) +
  coord_fixed()

print(p2)
ggsave("asymmetry_values_matrix.png", plot = p2, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

```

```{r, inspection}
# — assume dissimilarity_stats and unique_pairs already exist — 

# Pick one unordered pair to inspect (or loop over several)
up <- unique_pairs[1]
# e.g. up <- "2_45_vs_16_225"

parts <- strsplit(up, "_vs_")[[1]]
key_A <- paste(parts[1], parts[2], sep = "_vs_")
key_B <- paste(parts[2], parts[1], sep = "_vs_")

# 1) Per-participant A means (should be one rating per person, unless duplicates exist)
dfA <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
  filter(pair_key == key_A) %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  summarize(val_A = mean(dissimilarity), .groups = "drop")

# 2) Per-participant B means
dfB <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
  filter(pair_key == key_B) %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  summarize(val_B = mean(dissimilarity), .groups = "drop")

# 3) Join to see paired values
dfAB <- inner_join(dfA, dfB, by = "participant")

# 4) Also pull the raw trial rows that contributed to A and B
raw_trials <- dissimilarity_stats %>%
  filter(pair_key %in% c(key_A, key_B)) %>%
```
