```{r, big corr}
vec_cor_sym_matrix <- as.vector(precor_sym_matrix)
vec_cor_sym_matrix <- na.omit(vec_cor_sym_matrix)


# Function to prepare matrix: Set diagonal to NA, convert to vector, remove NAs
prepare_matrix <- function(mat) {
  diag(mat) <- NA  # Set diagonal to NA
  vec <- as.vector(mat)  # Convert matrix to vector
  vec <- na.omit(vec)  # Remove NAs
  return(vec)
}

results <- list()

# Loop through each mouse and their states
for (mouse_id in 1:9) {
  for (state in c("awake", "anaesthetised")) {
    # Construct matrix name with new naming convention
    mat_name <- paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "_", state, "_combined_distmatrix", sep = "")
    
    # Get the matrix from the environment
    mat <- get(mat_name)
    
    # Prepare the matrix
    prepared_vec <- prepare_matrix(mat)
    
    # Compute Spearman's correlation with the human data vector
    corr_result <- cor.test(prepared_vec, vec_cor_sym_matrix, method = "spearman")
    
    # Store the results
    results[[paste("Human x Mouse", mouse_id, state)]] <- c(corr_result$estimate, corr_result$p.value)
    
    # Print the correlation value and p-value
    cat("Spearman's correlation for Human x Mouse", mouse_id, state, ":", 
        "\nCorrelation: ", corr_result$estimate, "\nP-value: ", corr_result$p.value, "\n\n")
  }
  
  # Compute and print the correlation between awake and anaesthetised states for the same mouse
  awake_mat_name <- paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "_awake_combined_distmatrix", sep = "")
  anaesthetised_mat_name <- paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "_anaesthetised_combined_distmatrix", sep = "")
  
  awake_vec <- prepare_matrix(get(awake_mat_name))
  anaesthetised_vec <- prepare_matrix(get(anaesthetised_mat_name))
  
  corr_result_state <- cor.test(awake_vec, anaesthetised_vec, method = "spearman")
  
  # Store the results
  results[[paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "Awake x Anaesthetised")]] <- c(corr_result_state$estimate, corr_result_state$p.value)
  
  # Print the correlation value and p-value
  cat("Spearman's correlation for Mouse", mouse_id, "Awake x Anaesthetised:", 
      "\nCorrelation: ", corr_result_state$estimate, "\nP-value: ", corr_result_state$p.value, "\n\n")
}

results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(results), function(x) {
  if(grepl("Human x Mouse", x)) { # Include only human vs mouse correlations
    data.frame(Name = x,
               Correlation = abs(results[[x]][1]), # Take absolute value
               State = ifelse(grepl("awake", x, ignore.case = TRUE), "Awake", "Anaesthetised"),
               MouseID = gsub("Human x Mouse |awake|anaesthetised", "", x, ignore.case = TRUE))
  }
})) %>%
  na.omit() %>% # Remove NAs resulting from the conditional inclusion
  mutate(MouseID = as.numeric(MouseID), # Convert MouseID to numeric
         State = factor(State, levels = c("Awake", "Anaesthetised")))

# Plotting
cor_plot <- ggplot(results_df, aes(x = State, y = Correlation)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5,# Set alpha for boxplot to make it slightly transparent
               stat = "boxplot", lwd = 0.8, # Line width for the boxes
               coef = 0, width = 0.3) + 
  geom_jitter(aes(color = ifelse(MouseID == 0, "Combined Mice", "Individual Mice")), width = 0) + 
  geom_line(data = results_df %>% group_by(MouseID) %>% mutate(Group = 1), # Ensure lines are drawn for each mouse
            aes(group = interaction(MouseID, Group), color = ifelse(MouseID == 0, "Combined Mice", "Individual Mice")), alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Combined Mice" = "red", "Individual Mice" = "black")) +
  labs(title = "",
       y = "Spearman's Correlation Coefficient to Human", x = "") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right") + # Hide the legend title
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.05)) 

# Addition of significance markers 
max_correlation <- max(results_df$Correlation, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.05  # Adding a small margin for clarity

cor_plot <- cor_plot +
  geom_signif(comparisons = list(c("Awake", "Anaesthetised")), 
              map_signif_level = TRUE,  # This automatically maps significance levels (e.g., *** for p < 0.001)
              y_position = max_correlation,
              tip_length = 0.05)

ggsave("Combined_Cor_plot.png", plot = cor_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)


sapply(split(results_df$Correlation, results_df$State), function(x) {
  cat("\nState:", names(x)[1], "\n")
  cat("Median: ", median(x), "\n")
  cat("1st Quartile: ", quantile(x, 0.25), "\n")
  cat("3rd Quartile: ", quantile(x, 0.75), "\n")
})

test_results <- wilcox.test(Correlation ~ State, data = results_df)

# Printing the results of the test
cat("Wilcoxon rank sum test results:\n")
cat("W statistic: ", test_results$statistic, "\n")
cat("P-value: ", test_results$p.value, "\n")
if (test_results$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("The difference between the medians is statistically significant.\n")
} else {
  cat("There is no significant difference between the medians.\n")
}

cor_plot

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Use only individual mice (drop MouseID == 0 "Combined")
df <- results_df %>% filter(MouseID != 0)

# Wide layout: one row per mouse, one column per state
w <- df %>%
  select(MouseID, State, Correlation) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = State, values_from = Correlation)

# Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test (Awake vs Anaesthetised within mouse)
wilcox.test(w$Awake, w$Anaesthetised, paired = TRUE, alternative = "two.sided",
            conf.int = TRUE)



```
## Correlation final plot for supplementary
```{r, corr for pub}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggsignif)
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

precor_sym_matrix <- sym_dissimilarity_matrix 
diag(precor_sym_matrix) <- NA
vec_cor_sym_matrix <- as.vector(precor_sym_matrix)
vec_cor_sym_matrix <- na.omit(vec_cor_sym_matrix)

# Function to prepare matrix: Set diagonal to NA, convert to vector, remove NAs
prepare_matrix <- function(mat) {
  diag(mat) <- NA
  vec <- as.vector(mat)
  vec <- na.omit(vec)
  return(vec)
}

results <- list()

# Loop through each mouse and their states
for (mouse_id in 1:9) {
  for (state in c("awake", "anaesthetised")) {
    mat_name <- paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "_", state, "_combined_distmatrix", sep = "")
    mat <- get(mat_name)
    prepared_vec <- prepare_matrix(mat)
    corr_result <- cor.test(prepared_vec, vec_cor_sym_matrix, method = "spearman")
    results[[paste("Human x Mouse", mouse_id, state)]] <- c(corr_result$estimate, corr_result$p.value)
    cat("Spearman's correlation for Human x Mouse", mouse_id, state, ":", 
        "\nCorrelation: ", corr_result$estimate, "\nP-value: ", corr_result$p.value, "\n\n")
  }
  
  awake_mat_name <- paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "_awake_combined_distmatrix", sep = "")
  anaesthetised_mat_name <- paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "_anaesthetised_combined_distmatrix", sep = "")
  awake_vec <- prepare_matrix(get(awake_mat_name))
  anaesthetised_vec <- prepare_matrix(get(anaesthetised_mat_name))
  corr_result_state <- cor.test(awake_vec, anaesthetised_vec, method = "spearman")
  results[[paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "Awake x Anaesthetised")]] <- c(corr_result_state$estimate, corr_result_state$p.value)
  cat("Spearman's correlation for Mouse", mouse_id, "Awake x Anaesthetised:", 
      "\nCorrelation: ", corr_result_state$estimate, "\nP-value: ", corr_result_state$p.value, "\n\n")
}

results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(results), function(x) {
  if (grepl("Human x Mouse", x)) {
    data.frame(Name = x,
               Correlation = abs(results[[x]][1]),
               State = ifelse(grepl("awake", x, ignore.case = TRUE), "Awake", "Anaesthetised"),
               MouseID = gsub("Human x Mouse |awake|anaesthetised", "", x, ignore.case = TRUE))
  }
})) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  mutate(MouseID = as.numeric(MouseID),
         State = factor(State, levels = c("Awake", "Anaesthetised")))

# ---- Color mapping ----
results_df <- results_df %>%
  mutate(MouseID = as.integer(MouseID),
         MouseID_label = ifelse(MouseID == 0, "Combined", paste0("Mouse ", MouseID)))

mouse_ids <- sort(unique(results_df$MouseID[results_df$MouseID != 0]))
mouse_levels <- c(if (any(results_df$MouseID == 0)) "Combined" else character(0),
                  paste0("Mouse ", mouse_ids))
results_df$MouseID_label <- factor(results_df$MouseID_label, levels = mouse_levels)

palette_mice <- scales::hue_pal(l = 70, c = 100)(length(mouse_ids))
names(palette_mice) <- paste0("Mouse ", mouse_ids)
pal <- palette_mice
if (any(results_df$MouseID == 0)) {
  pal <- c(Combined = "grey40", pal)
}

# ---- Plotting ----
cor_plot <- ggplot(results_df, aes(x = State, y = Correlation)) +
  # Boxplot first (background)
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5, stat = "boxplot", lwd = 0.8, coef = 0, width = 0.3) +
  # Thicker connecting lines per mouse (still behind points)
  geom_line(
    data = results_df %>% filter(MouseID != 0) %>% arrange(MouseID, State),
    aes(group = MouseID_label, color = MouseID_label),
    alpha = 0.9, size = 1.4
  ) +
  # >>> POINTS LAST so they appear IN FRONT of the boxplot <<<
  geom_point(
    aes(color = MouseID_label),
    size = 3.4, alpha = 0.95, shape = 16
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal, name = "Mouse ID") +
  labs(y = "Spearman's Correlation Coefficient to Human", x = NULL) +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.05))

# Significance markers
max_correlation <- max(results_df$Correlation, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.05
cor_plot <- cor_plot +
  geom_signif(comparisons = list(c("Awake", "Anaesthetised")), 
              map_signif_level = TRUE,
              y_position = max_correlation,
              tip_length = 0.05)

ggsave("Combined_Cor_plot.png", plot = cor_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Summary stats
sapply(split(results_df$Correlation, results_df$State), function(x) {
  cat("\nState:", names(x)[1], "\n")
  cat("Median: ", median(x), "\n")
  cat("1st Quartile: ", quantile(x, 0.25), "\n")
  cat("3rd Quartile: ", quantile(x, 0.75), "\n")
})

# Paired Wilcoxon
df <- results_df %>% filter(MouseID != 0)
w <- df %>% select(MouseID, State, Correlation) %>% pivot_wider(names_from = State, values_from = Correlation)
wilcox.test(w$Awake, w$Anaesthetised, paired = TRUE, alternative = "two.sided", conf.int = TRUE)

cor_plot

```
##Correlation plots for excitatory mice x humans
```{r, comb excite corr}
precor_sym_matrix <- sym_dissimilarity_matrix 
diag(precor_sym_matrix) <- NA
vec_cor_sym_matrix <- as.vector(precor_sym_matrix)
vec_cor_sym_matrix <- na.omit(vec_cor_sym_matrix)


# Function to prepare matrix: Set diagonal to NA, convert to vector, remove NAs
prepare_matrix <- function(mat) {
  diag(mat) <- NA  # Set diagonal to NA
  vec <- as.vector(mat)  # Convert matrix to vector
  vec <- na.omit(vec)  # Remove NAs
  return(vec)
}

results <- list()

# Loop through each mouse and their states
for (mouse_id in 0:9) {
  for (state in c("awake", "anaesthetised")) {
    # Construct matrix name with new naming convention
    mat_name <- paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "_", state, "_excitatory_distmatrix", sep = "")
    
    # Get the matrix from the environment
    mat <- get(mat_name)
    
    # Prepare the matrix
    prepared_vec <- prepare_matrix(mat)
    
    # Compute Spearman's correlation with the human data vector
    corr_result <- cor.test(prepared_vec, vec_cor_sym_matrix, method = "spearman")
    
    # Store the results
    results[[paste("Human x Excitatory Mouse", mouse_id, state)]] <- c(corr_result$estimate, corr_result$p.value)
    
    # Print the correlation value and p-value
    cat("Spearman's correlation for Human x Excitatory Mouse", mouse_id, state, ":", 
        "\nCorrelation: ", corr_result$estimate, "\nP-value: ", corr_result$p.value, "\n\n")
  }
  
  # Compute and print the correlation between awake and anaesthetised states for the same mouse
  awake_mat_name <- paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "_awake_excitatory_distmatrix", sep = "")
  anaesthetised_mat_name <- paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "_anaesthetised_excitatory_distmatrix", sep = "")
  
  awake_vec <- prepare_matrix(get(awake_mat_name))
  anaesthetised_vec <- prepare_matrix(get(anaesthetised_mat_name))
  
  corr_result_state <- cor.test(awake_vec, anaesthetised_vec, method = "spearman")
  
  # Store the results
  results[[paste("Mouse", mouse_id, "Awake x Anaesthetised")]] <- c(corr_result_state$estimate, corr_result_state$p.value)
  
  # Print the correlation value and p-value
  cat("Spearman's correlation for Excitatory Mouse", mouse_id, "Awake x Anaesthetised:", 
      "\nCorrelation: ", corr_result_state$estimate, "\nP-value: ", corr_result_state$p.value, "\n\n")
}

results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(results), function(x) {
  if(grepl("Human x Excitatory Mouse", x)) { # Include only human vs mouse correlations
    data.frame(Name = x,
               Correlation = abs(results[[x]][1]), # Take absolute value
               State = ifelse(grepl("awake", x, ignore.case = TRUE), "Awake", "Anaesthetised"),
               MouseID = gsub("Human x Excitatory Mouse |awake|anaesthetised", "", x, ignore.case = TRUE))
  }
})) %>%
  na.omit() %>% # Remove NAs resulting from the conditional inclusion
  mutate(MouseID = as.numeric(MouseID), # Convert MouseID to numeric
         State = factor(State, levels = c("Awake", "Anaesthetised")))

# Plotting
cor_plot <- ggplot(results_df, aes(x = State, y = Correlation)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA, # Hide outliers # Set alpha for boxplot to make it slightly transparent
               stat = "boxplot", lwd = 0.8, # Line width for the boxes
               coef = 0) + 
  geom_jitter(aes(color = ifelse(MouseID == 0, "Combined Mice", "Individual Mice")), width = 0) + # Use geom_jitter for individual points to prevent overlap
  geom_line(data = results_df %>% group_by(MouseID) %>% mutate(Group = 1), # Ensure lines are drawn for each mouse
            aes(group = interaction(MouseID, Group), color = ifelse(MouseID == 0, "Combined Mice", "Individual Mice")), alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Combined Mice" = "red", "Individual Mice" = "black")) +
  labs(title = "",
       y = "Spearman's Correlation Coefficient to Human", x = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) + # Hide the legend title
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.05)) 

ggsave("Excitatory_Cor_plot.png", plot = cor_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)

cor_plot

```

##Correlation for SF / dir between mice awk x ans 
```{r, awkxans SF}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggsignif)
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)

# Function to prepare matrix: Set diagonal to NA, convert to vector, remove NAs
prepare_matrix <- function(mat) {
  diag(mat) <- NA  # Set diagonal to NA
  vec <- as.vector(mat)  # Convert matrix to vector
  vec <- na.omit(vec)  # Remove NAs
  return(vec)
}

# Initialize vectors to store correlation coefficients
correlations_SF <- numeric(10)
correlations_dir <- numeric(10)

# Compute correlations for each mouse
for (i in 0:9) {
  # Spatial frequency matrices
  mat_awake_SF <- get(paste0("mouse", i, "_SF_distmatrix_awk"))
  mat_anesth_SF <- get(paste0("mouse", i, "_SF_distmatrix_ans"))
  vec_awake_SF <- prepare_matrix(mat_awake_SF)
  vec_anesth_SF <- prepare_matrix(mat_anesth_SF)
  correlations_SF[i+1] <- cor(vec_awake_SF, vec_anesth_SF, method = "spearman")
  
  # Direction matrices
  mat_awake_dir <- get(paste0("mouse", i, "_dir_distmatrix_awk"))
  mat_anesth_dir <- get(paste0("mouse", i, "_dir_distmatrix_ans"))
  vec_awake_dir <- prepare_matrix(mat_awake_dir)
  vec_anesth_dir <- prepare_matrix(mat_anesth_dir)
  correlations_dir[i+1] <- cor(vec_awake_dir, vec_anesth_dir, method = "spearman")
}

# Create a data frame for plotting
df <- data.frame(
  Mouse = rep(0:9, 2),
  Correlation = c(correlations_SF, correlations_dir),
  Stimulus = rep(c("Spatial Frequency", "Direction"), each = 10)
)

# Plotting using ggplot2

wilcox_test <- wilcox.test(df$Correlation[df$Stimulus == "Spatial Frequency"],
                           df$Correlation[df$Stimulus == "Direction"],
                           alternative = "two.sided")

# Create a factor for colors and labels, special handling for Mouse 0
df$MouseFactor <- as.factor(df$Mouse)
levels(df$MouseFactor) <- paste("Mouse", 0:9)
levels(df$MouseFactor)[1] <- "Combined Mice"

# Manually define muted colors
muted_colors <- c("#6baed6", "#74c476", "#9e9ac9", "#9e6ac1", "#969696", 
                  "#bcdbd9", "#dbdb8d", "#a1d99b", "#bdbdbd")


# Define colors for points and lines, with red for Mouse 0 and muted for others
mouse_colors <- c("red", muted_colors)  # Red for Mouse 0, muted colors for others

# Plotting
cor_plot <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Stimulus, y = Correlation, group = MouseFactor)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = MouseFactor), size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.2)) +  # Increase point size
  geom_line(aes(color = MouseFactor), position = position_dodge(width = 0.2)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = mouse_colors) +
  geom_signif(comparisons = list(c("Spatial Frequency", "Direction")),
              map_signif_level = TRUE,
              textsize = 4, vjust = -0.2) +
  labs(title = "Correlation Between Awake and Anaesthetised States for Each Mouse",
       x = "Stimulus Type", y = "Spearman Correlation Coefficient",
       color = "Mouse Number") +  # Legend title
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")  # Move legend to the right

# Save the plot
ggsave("Cor_plot_SFxDir.png", plot = cor_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)

cor_plot

```




## Correlation to human over time figure
```{r,better cor plots}
# Load the required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)

# Filter data for the "combined" DataType
filtered_data <- correlation_data %>%
  filter(MouseID != 0, DataType == "combined") # Exclude mouse 0 (pooled) 

# Create the plot
plot <- ggplot(filtered_data, aes(x = TimeInSeconds, y = Correlation, color = Condition)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", aes(group = Condition)) +  # Ensure only lines are plotted
  #stat_summary(fun.data = mean_cl_boot, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.03, aes(group = Condition)) +  # Optional: Add error bars
  geom_boxplot(aes(group = interaction(TimeInSeconds, Condition), fill = Condition), alpha = 0.5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.01)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    stat = "boxplot", 
    aes(group = interaction(TimeInSeconds, Condition), color = Condition),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.01),
    width = 0.05  # Adjust this to change the width of the error bar caps
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Anaesthetised" = "skyblue", "Awake" = "pink")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Anaesthetised" = "blue", "Awake" = "red")) +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "Time in Seconds",
       y = "Spearman's Rank Correlation Coefficient to Human dissimilarity Matrix",
       color = "Condition",
       fill = "Condition") +
  theme_pubr()+
  theme(legend.position = "right")

# Print the plot
print(plot)

ggsave("All_mice_corr_over_time_Correlation_Plot.png", plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)


```

## _______________For pilot trials______________________
## __|| Run 'Packages' first to ensure proper loading! ||__

## Load data 
```{r trial load data}
# Load necessary libraries
library(data.table)

# Define the path to the main folder containing subfolders
main_folder <- "/Users/gleim/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/University/2023:24 Honours/Project Stats and R/Motion_analysis_KR/data/pilot_trials"

# Define the subfolder names
subfolders <- c("0.005_trial", "0.04_trial", "0.3_trial")

# Specify the columns to keep
columns_to_keep <- c("Prolific_ID*", "Prolific ID*", "circle1_ori", "circle2_ori", "similarity")

# Initialize an empty list to store data tables
trial_data_list <- list()

# Iterate over each subfolder and each file within the subfolder
for (subfolder in subfolders) {
  files <- list.files(file.path(main_folder, subfolder), pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
  
  for (file in files) {
    # Use fread to read the file, selecting only the specified columns
    data <- fread(file, select = columns_to_keep)
    
    # Check if 'Prolific_ID*' needs to be renamed to 'participant'
    if ("Prolific_ID*" %in% names(data)) {
      setnames(data, old = "Prolific_ID*", new = "participant")
    }
    if ("Prolific ID*" %in% names(data)) {
      setnames(data, old = "Prolific ID*", new = "participant")
    }
    
    # Remove NA values from specified columns
    data <- data[!is.na(circle1_ori) & !is.na(circle2_ori)]
    
    # Add a column indicating the trial type
    data[, trial_type := subfolder]
    
    # Add the current data table to the list
    trial_data_list[[length(trial_data_list) + 1]] <- data
  }
}

# Combine all data tables in the list into a single data table, ensuring columns match and filling missing values if necessary
combined_pilot_data <- rbindlist(trial_data_list, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# Print the combined data table to check
print(head(combined_pilot_data))

# Optional: Check the structure and summary of the combined data
str(combined_pilot_data)
summary(combined_pilot_data)

```


##Rescale to dissimilarity before correlation 
```{r, pre-rescale for pilot }
  # First rescale similarity to dissimilarity 
  str(combined_pilot_data)
  table(combined_pilot_data$similarity)
  
  rescale <- function(combined_pilot_data) {
  # Ensure that similarity is numeric
  if (!is.numeric(combined_pilot_data$similarity)) {
    combined_pilot_data$similarity <- as.numeric(as.character(combined_pilot_data$similarity))
  }
  
  # Multiply by -1
  combined_pilot_data$similarity <- combined_pilot_data$similarity * -1
  
  # Apply conditional adjustments
  combined_pilot_data$dissimilarity <- ifelse(combined_pilot_data$similarity >= 1, combined_pilot_data$similarity + 3, combined_pilot_data$similarity + 4)
  
  return(combined_pilot_data)
}

# Apply the function to combined_pilot_data
  
combined_pilot_data <- rescale(combined_pilot_data)

# FLip similarity back
combined_pilot_data$similarity <- combined_pilot_data$similarity * -1

```


## Double pass for pilot trials
```{r, DP for pilots and plot}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Define the double pass correlation cutoff
dp_cutoff <- 0.139

# No. of pairwise trials before duplication for double pass
n <- 210

# Function to calculate number of unique participants
n.participants <- function(df) {
  length(unique(df$participant))
}

# Function to calculate double-pass correlation
double.cor <- function(dissimilarity) {
  if(length(dissimilarity) != 420) {
    cat("Warning: dissimilarity vector does not have 420 elements\n")
  }
  
  first_pass <- dissimilarity[1:210]
  second_pass <- dissimilarity[211:420]
  
  # Check for constant data
  if(sd(first_pass) == 0 | sd(second_pass) == 0) {
    cat("Warning: one of the vectors is constant\n")
    return(NA)
  }
  
  result <- cor(first_pass, second_pass, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
  
  if(is.na(result)) {
    cat("NA correlation detected\n")
  }
  
  return(result)
}

# Calculate double-pass correlations for each participant
double.cor.summary <- combined_pilot_data %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  summarise(correlation = double.cor(dissimilarity))

# Subset based on correlation threshold
double.cor.acceptable <- filter(double.cor.summary, correlation >= dp_cutoff)
double.cor.unacceptable <- filter(double.cor.summary, correlation < dp_cutoff)

# Extract trial data for acceptable and unacceptable correlations
pilot_trialdata <- filter(combined_pilot_data, participant %in% double.cor.acceptable$participant)
pilot_lowdpdata <- filter(combined_pilot_data, participant %in% double.cor.unacceptable$participant)

# Plotting function for double-pass correlations
plot_double_pass_correlation <- function(data, title, cutoff = NULL) {
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(y = correlation, x = factor(1))) +
    geom_violin(trim = FALSE, fill = "skyblue", color = "blue") +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 2.5) +
    xlab(NULL) +  ylab("Spearman's Correlation") +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)) +
    theme_pubr() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "plain", size = 20),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain", margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0), hjust = 0.65),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 35, face = "plain", colour = "black"),
      axis.line = element_line(size = 1.5),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "white"),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#d6d6d6"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  if (!is.null(cutoff)) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = cutoff, linewidth = 1.5, colour = 'red', linetype = 'dashed') +
      annotate("text", x = 1.4, y = cutoff - 0.07, size = 10, label = paste("Cut-off:", cutoff), vjust = 0, colour = 'red')
  }
  
  return(p)
}

# Plot correlations for all data, acceptable, and unacceptable
p_all <- plot_double_pass_correlation(double.cor.summary, "DP Correlation - All Data", dp_cutoff)
ggsave("DP_Correlation_All_Data.png", plot = p_all, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

p_acceptable <- plot_double_pass_correlation(double.cor.acceptable, "DP Correlation - Acceptable", dp_cutoff)
ggsave("DP_Correlation_Acceptable_Data.png", plot = p_acceptable, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)


# Print number of participants in various datasets
cat("Participants - All Data:", n.participants(combined_pilot_data), "\n")
cat("Participants - Acceptable:", n.participants(double.cor.acceptable), "\n")
cat("Participants - Unacceptable:", n.participants(double.cor.unacceptable), "\n")

# Add trial type to double-pass summary
double.cor.summary <- combined_pilot_data %>%
  select(participant, trial_type) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  left_join(double.cor.summary, by = "participant")

# Categorize participants
double.cor.summary$Status <- ifelse(double.cor.summary$correlation >= dp_cutoff, "Acceptable", "Unacceptable")

# Count participants per trial type and status
trial_type_summary <- double.cor.summary %>%
  group_by(trial_type, Status) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Status, values_from = Count, values_fill = 0)

# Print summary
cat("Participant counts by trial type:\n")
print(trial_type_summary)

```
## Swap order
```{r, swap order}

pilot_trialdata <- pilot_trialdata %>%
  # 1) Number trials within each participant
  group_by(participant) %>%
  mutate(trial_rank = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  # 2) For trials >150, swap circle1↔circle2
  mutate(
    tmp_ori  = circle1_ori,
    circle1_ori = ifelse(trial_rank > 210, circle2_ori, circle1_ori),
    circle2_ori = ifelse(trial_rank > 210, tmp_ori, circle2_ori)
  ) %>%
  
  # 3) Clean up
  select(-tmp_ori, -trial_rank)
```


## Do everything
```{r, pilot with diagonal}
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(MASS)       # For MDS
library(ggimage)    # For adding icons to MDS plot
library(ggpubr)
library(ggsignif)
library(grid)       # For unit()

# Function to map orientation to index
map_orientation_to_index <- function(orientation) {
  as.integer(orientation / 18) + 1
}

# Function to calculate mean and SD for each diagonal (offset)
mean_diag_dissimilarity <- function(matrix) {
  max_dim <- dim(matrix)[1]  # assuming square
  diag_stats <- lapply(0:(max_dim - 1), function(k) {
    if (k == 0) {
      diag_elements <- matrix[row(matrix) == col(matrix)]
    } else {
      diag_elements <- c(
        matrix[row(matrix) == (col(matrix) - k)],
        matrix[row(matrix) == (col(matrix) + k)]
      )
    }
    data.frame(
      Diagonal         = k,
      MeanDissimilarity = mean(diag_elements, na.rm = TRUE),
      SD                = sd(diag_elements,   na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  })
  do.call(rbind, diag_stats)
}

# Per-participant dissimilarity & MDS
create_plot_dissimilarity_matrix <- function(pilot_trialdata, participant_id, trial_type) {
  dissimilarity_matrix <- matrix(NA, 20, 20)
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(pilot_trialdata))) {
    r <- map_orientation_to_index(pilot_trialdata$circle1_ori[i])
    c <- map_orientation_to_index(pilot_trialdata$circle2_ori[i])
    dissimilarity_matrix[r,c] <- pilot_trialdata$dissimilarity[i]
  }
  orientation_values <- seq(0, 342, by = 18)
  # heatmap
  df <- reshape2::melt(dissimilarity_matrix, varnames = c("Row","Column"))
  df$Row    <- orientation_values[df$Row]
  df$Column <- orientation_values[df$Column]
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(Row, Column, fill = value)) +
    geom_tile(color="white") +
    scale_fill_gradient(low="white", high="black", na.value="red",
      guide = guide_colourbar(title="Dissimilarity", reverse=TRUE,
        barwidth=unit(1.2,"in"), barheight=unit(2.4,"in")),
      breaks=0:7, labels=0:7) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks=orientation_values) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks=orientation_values) +
    coord_fixed() + theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(hjust=0,size=20),
      axis.title    = element_text(size=14),
      axis.text.x   = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1),
      legend.title  = element_text(size=16),
      legend.text   = element_text(size=16),
      panel.grid    = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(x="Circle 1 Direction", y="Circle 2 Direction",
         title=paste("Participant", participant_id, "-", trial_type))
  dir.create("pilot_trial_figres", showWarnings=FALSE)
  ggsave(sprintf("pilot_trial_figres/dissimilarity_matrix_participant_%s_%s.png",
                 participant_id, trial_type),
         p, width=10, height=10, dpi=300)

  # MDS
  dm <- dissimilarity_matrix
  dm <- (dm + t(dm))/2
  dm[is.na(dm)] <- 0
  mds <- cmdscale(as.dist(dm), eig=TRUE, k=2)
  mdf <- data.frame(Dim1=mds$points[,1], Dim2=mds$points[,2],
                    Orientation=orientation_values,
                    image_path=sprintf("Arrow_icons/%d.png", orientation_values))
  p_mds <- ggplot(mdf, aes(Dim1, Dim2)) +
    geom_blank() + geom_image(aes(image=image_path), size=0.05) +
    coord_fixed() + theme_pubr() +
    theme(
      plot.title   = element_text(hjust=0.5,size=20),
      axis.title   = element_text(size=35),
      axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y  = element_text(size=35),
      axis.line    = element_line(size=1.5),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill="white"),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(color="#d6d6d6")
    ) +
    labs(title=paste("MDS Plot for Participant", participant_id, "-", trial_type),
         x="Dimension 1", y="Dimension 2")
  ggsave(sprintf("pilot_trial_figres/mds_plot_participant_%s_%s.png",
                 participant_id, trial_type),
         p_mds, width=8, height=8, dpi=300)
}

# Loop per participant
for(pid in unique(pilot_trialdata$participant)) {
  pdata <- subset(pilot_trialdata, participant==pid)
  tt <- unique(pdata$trial_type)
  if (length(tt)==1) {
    create_plot_dissimilarity_matrix(pdata, pid, tt)
  } else {
    warning(paste("Participant",pid,"has multiple trial types, skipping."))
  }
}

# Function to map orientation to index
map_orientation_to_index <- function(orientation) {
  as.integer(orientation / 18) + 1
}

# Function to calculate mean and SD for each angular difference
mean_angular_dissimilarity <- function(matrix) {
  max_dim <- dim(matrix)[1]
  angular_diffs <- seq(0, 180, by = 18)
  stats <- lapply(angular_diffs, function(deg_diff) {
    indices <- which(abs(row(matrix) - col(matrix)) %% max_dim == deg_diff / 18, arr.ind = TRUE)
    values <- matrix[indices]
    data.frame(
      AngularDifference = deg_diff,
      MeanDissimilarity = mean(values, na.rm = TRUE),
      SD                = sd(values, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  })
  do.call(rbind, stats)
}

# Main function
create_average_dissimilarity_matrix <- function(trialdata, trial_type) {
  library(smacof)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(reshape2)
  library(ggimage)
  library(ggpubr)

  dm <- matrix(0, 20, 20)
  cnt <- matrix(0, 20, 20)
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(trialdata))) {
    r <- map_orientation_to_index(trialdata$circle1_ori[i])
    c <- map_orientation_to_index(trialdata$circle2_ori[i])
    dm[r, c]  <- dm[r, c]  + trialdata$dissimilarity[i]
    cnt[r, c] <- cnt[r, c] + 1
  }
  avg_dm <- dm / cnt
  avg_dm[is.nan(avg_dm)] <- NA
  orientation_values <- seq(0, 342, by = 18)

  # Heatmap
  df <- reshape2::melt(avg_dm, varnames = c("Row", "Column"))
  df$Row <- orientation_values[df$Row]
  df$Column <- orientation_values[df$Column]
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(Row, Column, fill = value)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colours = c("white", "black"),
      limits = c(0, 7), values = scales::rescale(0:7),
      na.value = "red",
      guide = guide_colorbar(title = "Dissimilarity", ticks = TRUE, ticks.colour = "white", title.position = "top"),
      breaks = 0:7, labels = 0:7
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = orientation_values) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = orientation_values) +
    coord_fixed() + theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.title = element_text(size = 26),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
      legend.key.size = unit(3.2, "cm"),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 20),
      axis.text = element_text(colour = "black"),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(title = paste("Average Dissimilarity Matrix for", trial_type))
  ggsave(sprintf("pilot_trial_figres/average_dissimilarity_matrix_%s.png", trial_type),
         p, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

  # MDS using smacof
  sym_dm <- (avg_dm + t(avg_dm)) / 2
  sym_dm[is.na(sym_dm)] <- 0
  dist_obj <- as.dist(sym_dm)
  mds <- smacofSym(dist_obj, ndim = 2)

  mdf <- data.frame(Dim1 = mds$conf[, 1], Dim2 = mds$conf[, 2],
                    Orientation = orientation_values,
                    image_path = sprintf("Arrow_icons/%d.png", orientation_values))

  p_mds <- ggplot(mdf, aes(Dim1, Dim2)) +
    geom_blank() + geom_image(aes(image = image_path), size = 0.08) +
    coord_fixed() +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-1, 0, 1), labels = c("-1", "0", "1"), limits = c(-1, 1)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(-1,0, 1), labels = c("-1", "0", "1"), limits = c(-1, 1)) +
    theme_pubr() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 45),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 40, colour = "black"),
      axis.line = element_line(size = 1.5),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "white"),
      panel.grid = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(x = "Dimension 1", y = "Dimension 2")
  ggsave(sprintf("pilot_trial_figres/mds_plot_average_%s.png", trial_type),
         p_mds, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

  # Diagonal analysis using angular difference
  diag_df <- mean_angular_dissimilarity(avg_dm)
  diag_df$Group <- "Mixed"
  diag_df$Group[diag_df$AngularDifference == 0]   <- "Same"
  diag_df$Group[diag_df$AngularDifference == 180] <- "Opposite"

  # Plot angular difference scatter
  p_diag <- ggplot(diag_df, aes(AngularDifference, MeanDissimilarity, color = Group)) +
    geom_point(size = 6) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = MeanDissimilarity - SD, ymax = MeanDissimilarity + SD), width = 4) +
    labs(x = "Angular Difference (°)", y = "Mean Dissimilarity") +
    theme_pubr() +
    theme(
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 45),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 45),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 40, colour = "black"),
      axis.line = element_line(size = 1.5),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "white"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none"
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 7), breaks = 0:7) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 90, 180), labels = c(0, 90, 180)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Opposite" = "red", "Same" = "blue", "Mixed" = "purple"))
  ggsave(sprintf("pilot_trial_figres/mean_dissimilarity_by_angle_%s.png", trial_type),
         p_diag, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
}

# Loop over trial types
for (tt in unique(pilot_trialdata$trial_type)) {
  subset_data <- subset(pilot_trialdata, trial_type == tt)
  create_average_dissimilarity_matrix(subset_data, tt)
}


```
## Pilot stats
```{r, pilot stats}

library(dplyr)

# Function to compute angular difference (0°–180°)
angular_diff <- function(a, b) {
  diff <- abs(a - b) %% 360
  pmin(diff, 360 - diff)
}

# Add angular difference column
pilot_trialdata$angular_diff <- angular_diff(pilot_trialdata$circle1_ori, pilot_trialdata$circle2_ori)

# Define trial types
trial_types <- c("0.005_trial", "0.04_trial", "0.3_trial")

# Loop over trial types
for (tt in trial_types) {
  cat("\nTrial Type:", tt, "\n")
  subset_data <- subset(pilot_trialdata, trial_type == tt)

  # Compute participant-level means for each condition
  df_180 <- subset(subset_data, angular_diff == 180) %>%
    group_by(participant) %>%
    summarise(mean_180 = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE))

  df_0 <- subset(subset_data, angular_diff == 0) %>%
    group_by(participant) %>%
    summarise(mean_0 = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE))

  df_less_than_180 <- subset(subset_data, angular_diff < 180 & angular_diff != 0) %>%
    group_by(participant) %>%
    summarise(mean_lt180 = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE))

  # Merge all three
  merged <- reduce(list(df_180, df_0, df_less_than_180), full_join, by = "participant")

  # Descriptive stats
  cat("Mean dissimilarity at 180°:", mean(merged$mean_180, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("SD at 180°:", sd(merged$mean_180, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

  # Paired t-test: 180° vs 0°
  if (sum(!is.na(merged$mean_180) & !is.na(merged$mean_0)) >= 2) {
    ttest_180_vs_0 <- t.test(merged$mean_180, merged$mean_0, paired = TRUE)
    cat("Paired t-test (180° vs 0°):\n")
    print(ttest_180_vs_0)
  } else {
    cat("Not enough paired data for 180° vs 0°\n")
  }

  # Paired t-test: 180° vs <180°
  if (sum(!is.na(merged$mean_180) & !is.na(merged$mean_lt180)) >= 2) {
    ttest_180_vs_lt180 <- t.test(merged$mean_180, merged$mean_lt180, paired = TRUE)
    cat("Paired t-test (180° vs <180°):\n")
    print(ttest_180_vs_lt180)
  } else {
    cat("Not enough paired data for 180° vs <180°\n")
  }
}

```


```{r, pilot asym}
library(dplyr)

# Function to compute angular difference
angular_diff <- function(a, b) {
  diff <- abs(a - b) %% 360
  pmin(diff, 360 - diff)
}

# Add angular difference column
pilot_trialdata$angular_diff <- angular_diff(pilot_trialdata$circle1_ori, pilot_trialdata$circle2_ori)

# Define trial types and symmetric pairs
trial_types <- c("0.005_trial", "0.04_trial", "0.3_trial")
angle_pairs <- list(c(18, 162), c(36, 144), c(54, 126), c(72, 108))

# Loop through trial types and angle pairs
for (tt in trial_types) {
  cat("\nTrial Type:", tt, "\n")
  data_tt <- filter(pilot_trialdata, trial_type == tt)
  
  for (pair in angle_pairs) {
    a1 <- pair[1]
    a2 <- pair[2]
    
    df_a1 <- filter(data_tt, angular_diff == a1) %>%
      group_by(participant) %>%
      summarise(mean_a1 = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE))
    
    df_a2 <- filter(data_tt, angular_diff == a2) %>%
      group_by(participant) %>%
      summarise(mean_a2 = mean(dissimilarity, na.rm = TRUE))
    
    merged <- inner_join(df_a1, df_a2, by = "participant")
    
    if (nrow(merged) >= 2) {
      cat(sprintf("Paired t-test for %d° vs %d°:\n", a1, a2))
      print(t.test(merged$mean_a1, merged$mean_a2, paired = TRUE))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("Not enough data for %d° vs %d° comparison.\n", a1, a2))
    }
  }
}

```
